question.english: Should all students pay for their university education?
question.chinese: 所有學生都應該為自己的大學教育付費嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Should all students pay for their university education?
所有學生都應該為自己的大學教育付費嗎？

components.english: Personally, I do not think all students should have to pay the full cost of university education by themselves.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我並不認為所有學生都應該自己承擔大學教育的全部費用。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I do not think all students should have to pay the full cost of
university education by themselves.
[就我個人而言，我並不認為所有學生都應該自己承擔大學教育的全部費用。]

components.english: The main reason is that higher education is not only a private benefit; it also benefits society as a whole. When someone becomes a doctor, teacher, engineer, or social worker, the value does not stop with that individual. It spreads outward. So, if university becomes something only the wealthy can comfortably afford, society may end up wasting a great deal of talent. In my view, education should be more like a ladder than a locked gate.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，高等教育不只是對個人有益；它同時也會讓整個社會受益。當一個人成為醫生、老師、工程師，或者社工時，那份價值並不只停留在他自己身上，而是會向外擴散。所以，如果大學變成只有富裕家庭才能輕鬆負擔的東西，社會最後很可能會白白浪費很多人才。在我看來，教育應該更像一把梯子，而不是一道上了鎖的門。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that higher education is not only a private
benefit; it also benefits society as a whole. When someone becomes a doctor,
teacher, engineer, or social worker, the value does not stop with that individual.
It spreads outward. So, if university becomes something only the wealthy can
comfortably afford, society may end up wasting a great deal of talent. In my
view, education should be more like a ladder than a locked gate.
[主要原因是，高等教育不只是對個人有益；它同時也會讓整個社會受益。當一個人成為醫
生、老師、工程師，或者社工時，那份價值並不只停留在他自己身上，而是會向外擴散。所以
，如果大學變成只有富裕家庭才能輕鬆負擔的東西，社會最後很可能會白白浪費很多人才。
在我看來，教育應該更像一把梯子，而不是一道上了鎖的門。]

components.english: For example, a very capable student from a low-income family may have the grades and the ability to do well at university, but if the cost is too high, that student may give up and go straight into work. In that case, it is not only the student who loses out; society loses out as well.
components.chinese: 例如，一位來自基層家庭、但非常有能力的學生，也許成績很好，也完全有能力讀大學；可是一旦學費太高，他就可能只好放棄，直接投入工作。在這種情況下，失去機會的不只是那位學生，整個社會其實也同樣有損失。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a very capable student from a low-income family may
have the grades and the ability to do well at university, but if the cost is too
high, that student may give up and go straight into work. In that case, it is not
only the student who loses out; society loses out as well.
[例如，一位來自基層家庭、但非常有能力的學生，也許成績很好，也完全有能力讀大學；可
是一旦學費太高，他就可能只好放棄，直接投入工作。在這種情況下，失去機會的不只是那
位學生，整個社會其實也同樣有損失。]

components.english: So, for that reason, I would say university education should not depend entirely on whether a student’s family can afford the bill.
components.chinese: 所以，基於這個原因，我會說，大學教育不應該完全取決於一個學生的家庭是否付得起那筆費用。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, for that reason, I would say university education should not
depend entirely on whether a student’s family can afford the bill.
[所以，基於這個原因，我會說，大學教育不應該完全取決於一個學生的家庭是否付得起那
筆費用。]

components.english: That said, I also do not think university should automatically be completely free for everyone, because students who can afford to contribute probably should.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為大學教育就一定要對所有人完全免費，因為那些有能力負擔一部分費用的學生，某程度上也應該作出貢獻。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also do not think university should automatically be
completely free for everyone, because students who can afford to contribute
probably should.
[不過，我也不認為大學教育就一定要對所有人完全免費，因為那些有能力負擔一部分費用
的學生，某程度上也應該作出貢獻。]

components.english: University costs money to run, and that money has to come from somewhere. If the government pays for absolutely everything, the system can become heavy and difficult to sustain. So, to me, the fairest approach is a balanced one: students from wealthier backgrounds can pay more, while those from poorer families should receive scholarships, subsidies, or financial support. In other words, the burden should be shared, but it should not fall with the same weight on every shoulder.
components.chinese: 大學本身需要資源去運作，而那些資源始終要有人負擔。如果政府把所有費用都全數包辦，整個制度也可能會變得沉重，長遠而言未必容易維持。所以，對我來說，最公平的做法是一種平衡式安排：來自較富裕家庭的學生可以付多一些，而家庭條件較差的學生則應該得到獎學金、資助，或者其他財政支援。換句話說，負擔可以共同分擔，但不應該以同樣重量壓在每一個人肩上。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: University costs money to run, and that money has to come
from somewhere. If the government pays for absolutely everything, the system
can become heavy and difficult to sustain. So, to me, the fairest approach is a
balanced one: students from wealthier backgrounds can pay more, while those
from poorer families should receive scholarships, subsidies, or financial support.
In other words, the burden should be shared, but it should not fall with the
same weight on every shoulder.
[大學本身需要資源去運作，而那些資源始終要有人負擔。如果政府把所有費用都全數包辦
，整個制度也可能會變得沉重，長遠而言未必容易維持。所以，對我來說，最公平的做法是
一種平衡式安排：來自較富裕家庭的學生可以付多一些，而家庭條件較差的學生則應該得
到獎學金、資助，或者其他財政支援。換句話說，負擔可以共同分擔，但不應該以同樣重量
壓在每一個人肩上。]

components.english: For instance, one student may be perfectly able to pay part of the tuition without real hardship, while another may struggle even with basic living costs. Treating those two cases in exactly the same way may look equal on paper, but in real life it is not especially fair.
components.chinese: 例如，一位學生也許完全有能力支付部分學費，而不會真的感到吃力；但另一位學生卻可能連基本生活開支都已經很勉強。如果把這兩種情況一模一樣地處理，紙面上看起來好像很平等，但放到現實裡，其實未必真正公平。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, one student may be perfectly able to pay part of the
tuition without real hardship, while another may struggle even with basic living
costs. Treating those two cases in exactly the same way may look equal on paper,
but in real life it is not especially fair.
[例如，一位學生也許完全有能力支付部分學費，而不會真的感到吃力；但另一位學生卻可
能連基本生活開支都已經很勉強。如果把這兩種情況一模一樣地處理，紙面上看起來好像
很平等，但放到現實裡，其實未必真正公平。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say not all students should have to pay the full cost of university education. A fairer system is one in which students contribute if they can, but no one is priced out of higher education because of family income. Higher education should open doors, not quietly close them.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，並不是所有學生都應該承擔大學教育的全部費用。更公平的制度應該是：有能力的人可以作出貢獻，但不應該有人因為家庭收入而被高等教育拒於門外。高等教育應該是用來打開機會之門的，而不是悄悄把門關上。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say not all students should have to pay the full cost
of university education. A fairer system is one in which students contribute if
they can, but no one is priced out of higher education because of family income.
Higher education should open doors, not quietly close them.
[總結來說，我會說，並不是所有學生都應該承擔大學教育的全部費用。更公平的制度應該
是：有能力的人可以作出貢獻，但不應該有人因為家庭收入而被高等教育拒於門外。高等教
育應該是用來打開機會之門的，而不是悄悄把門關上。]

question.english: Can you compare the education your parents had with the education that you received?
question.chinese: 你可以比較一下你父母接受的教育和你自己接受的教育嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Can you compare the education your parents had with the education that
you received?
[你可以比較一下你父母接受的教育和你自己接受的教育嗎？]

components.english: Yes, I think there are some clear differences. My parents’ education seems to have been more rigid and exam-oriented, whereas mine has been broader and a little more flexible.
components.chinese: 是的，我認為兩者之間有一些很明顯的分別。我父母那一代的教育似乎更嚴格、更以考試為中心，而我自己所接受的教育則較廣闊，也稍微更有彈性。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I think there are some clear differences. My parents’ education
seems to have been more rigid and exam-oriented, whereas mine has been
broader and a little more flexible.
[是的，我認為兩者之間有一些很明顯的分別。我父母那一代的教育似乎更嚴格、更以考試
為中心，而我自己所接受的教育則較廣闊，也稍微更有彈性。]

components.english: From what they have told me, their schooling was often more teacher-centred, with a stronger focus on memorisation, discipline, and getting the right answer. Students were expected to listen, copy notes, and follow the rules without asking too many questions. By contrast, in my experience, there has been a bit more room for discussion, presentations, group work, and independent thinking. In that sense, their education was more about staying on the rails, while mine has been more about learning how to steer as well.
components.chinese: 按照他們跟我說的情況，他們那時候的學校教育往往更以老師為中心，也更着重背誦、紀律，以及給出正確答案。學生通常被期望安靜地聽、抄筆記，並且守規矩，而不會鼓勵他們問太多問題。相比之下，以我的經驗來說，我所接受的教育則多了一些討論、口頭報告、小組合作，以及獨立思考的空間。從這個角度來說，他們那一代的教育更像是教人沿着軌道走，而我這一代的教育則更像是除了走路之外，也要學會怎樣掌舵。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: From what they have told me, their schooling was often more
teacher-centred, with a stronger focus on memorisation, discipline, and getting
the right answer. Students were expected to listen, copy notes, and follow the
rules without asking too many questions. By contrast, in my experience, there
has been a bit more room for discussion, presentations, group work, and
independent thinking. In that sense, their education was more about staying on
the rails, while mine has been more about learning how to steer as well.
[按照他們跟我說的情況，他們那時候的學校教育往往更以老師為中心，也更着重背誦、紀
律，以及給出正確答案。學生通常被期望安靜地聽、抄筆記，並且守規矩，而不會鼓勵他們
問太多問題。相比之下，以我的經驗來說，我所接受的教育則多了一些討論、口頭報告、小
組合作，以及獨立思考的空間。從這個角度來說，他們那一代的教育更像是教人沿着軌道走
，而我這一代的教育則更像是除了走路之外，也要學會怎樣掌舵。]

components.english: For example, my parents often describe lessons that were heavily based on textbooks and repetition, whereas I have had to do projects, use online resources, and speak up more in class. So even when the subjects are similar, the learning style can feel quite different.
components.chinese: 例如，我父母常常會形容他們的課堂主要是依靠課本和重複操練，而我自己則需要做專題、使用網上資源，並且在課堂上更主動表達意見。所以，即使科目本身可能相似，學習方式卻可以有相當大的不同。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, my parents often describe lessons that were heavily
based on textbooks and repetition, whereas I have had to do projects, use online
resources, and speak up more in class. So even when the subjects are similar, the
learning style can feel quite different.
[例如，我父母常常會形容他們的課堂主要是依靠課本和重複操練，而我自己則需要做專
題、使用網上資源，並且在課堂上更主動表達意見。所以，即使科目本身可能相似，學習方
式卻可以有相當大的不同。]

components.english: So overall, I would say my education has been more varied and interactive, while my parents’ education seems to have been more structured and rule-driven.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我會說我所接受的教育較多元，也較有互動性；而我父母那一代的教育則似乎更有框架，也更受規則主導。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So overall, I would say my education has been more varied and
interactive, while my parents’ education seems to have been more structured and
rule-driven.
[所以，整體來說，我會說我所接受的教育較多元，也較有互動性；而我父母那一代的教育
則似乎更有框架，也更受規則主導。]

components.english: That said, I do not think my education is better in every way, because my parents’ generation probably had some strengths that are less common now.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為我所接受的教育在每一方面都比較好，因為我父母那一代的教育其實也很可能有一些現在較少見的優點。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think my education is better in every way, because
my parents’ generation probably had some strengths that are less common now.
[不過，我也不認為我所接受的教育在每一方面都比較好，因為我父母那一代的教育其實也
很可能有一些現在較少見的優點。]

components.english: One obvious strength was discipline. Their generation often had fewer distractions, less screen time, and a stronger habit of respecting teachers and concentrating for long stretches. These days, students may have more resources and more freedom, but they also have more noise around them. Sometimes we have the whole world at our fingertips and still struggle to keep our minds in one place. So, in a way, my parents may have had a narrower education, but perhaps a tougher and more focused one.
components.chinese: 其中一個很明顯的優點就是紀律感。他們那一代通常面對較少分心的東西、較少螢幕干擾，也更習慣尊重老師，以及長時間保持專注。今天的學生也許有更多資源，也有更多自由，但同時身邊也有更多雜音。有時我們明明把整個世界都握在手裡，卻反而更難把心神安放在同一件事上。所以，某程度上，我父母那一代接受的教育也許比較窄，但可能也更硬朗、更專注。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: One obvious strength was discipline. Their generation often
had fewer distractions, less screen time, and a stronger habit of respecting
teachers and concentrating for long stretches. These days, students may have
more resources and more freedom, but they also have more noise around them.
Sometimes we have the whole world at our fingertips and still struggle to keep
our minds in one place. So, in a way, my parents may have had a narrower
education, but perhaps a tougher and more focused one.
[其中一個很明顯的優點就是紀律感。他們那一代通常面對較少分心的東西、較少螢幕干擾
，也更習慣尊重老師，以及長時間保持專注。今天的學生也許有更多資源，也有更多自由，
但同時身邊也有更多雜音。有時我們明明把整個世界都握在手裡，卻反而更難把心神安放
在同一件事上。所以，某程度上，我父母那一代接受的教育也許比較窄，但可能也更硬朗、
更專注。]

components.english: For instance, my parents often say that when they studied, they simply sat down and got on with it, because there were fewer distractions pulling them in ten different directions. By contrast, students today may have laptops, phones, and endless online materials, which are useful, but can also make focus slippery.
components.chinese: 例如，我父母常常說，他們當年讀書時就是坐下來便專心去做，因為那時沒有那麼多東西把他們的注意力拉向不同方向。相比之下，今天的學生雖然有手提電腦、手機，以及無窮無盡的網上資料，這些東西固然有用，但同時也會令專注力變得更容易流走。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, my parents often say that when they studied, they
simply sat down and got on with it, because there were fewer distractions
pulling them in ten different directions. By contrast, students today may have
laptops, phones, and endless online materials, which are useful, but can also
make focus slippery.
[例如，我父母常常說，他們當年讀書時就是坐下來便專心去做，因為那時沒有那麼多東西
把他們的注意力拉向不同方向。相比之下，今天的學生雖然有手提電腦、手機，以及無窮無
盡的網上資料，這些東西固然有用，但同時也會令專注力變得更容易流走。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say my parents’ education was probably stricter and more disciplined, while mine has been more modern, flexible, and wide-ranging. If I had to sum it up, I’d say their education built strong foundations, while mine has tried to build both foundations and flexibility.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說我父母那一代的教育大概更嚴格，也更有紀律，而我這一代所接受的教育則更現代化、更有彈性，也更廣泛。如果要我簡單總結，我會說他們那一代的教育是在打穩地基，而我這一代的教育則是在打地基的同時，也嘗試培養彈性。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say my parents’ education was probably stricter and
more disciplined, while mine has been more modern, flexible, and wide-ranging.
If I had to sum it up, I’d say their education built strong foundations, while
mine has tried to build both foundations and flexibility.
[總結來說，我會說我父母那一代的教育大概更嚴格，也更有紀律，而我這一代所接受的教
育則更現代化、更有彈性，也更廣泛。如果要我簡單總結，我會說他們那一代的教育是在打
穩地基，而我這一代的教育則是在打地基的同時，也嘗試培養彈性。]

question.english: What changes do you think will happen in the classrooms of the near future?
question.chinese: 你認為在不久的將來，課室裡會出現甚麼改變？

source_lines.text: What changes do you think will happen in the classrooms of the near future?
你認為在不久的將來，課室裡會出現甚麼改變？

components.english: If I had to guess, classrooms in the near future will probably become more digital, more interactive, and a little less dependent on traditional chalk-and-talk teaching.
components.chinese: 如果要我猜，我覺得未來不久的課室大概會變得更數碼化、更有互動性，也會稍微減少那種很傳統、由老師單向講授的教學模式。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: If I had to guess, classrooms in the near future will probably become
more digital, more interactive, and a little less dependent on traditional
chalk-and-talk teaching.
[如果要我猜，我覺得未來不久的課室大概會變得更數碼化、更有互動性，也會稍微減少那
種很傳統、由老師單向講授的教學模式。]

components.english: The reason is that technology is already changing the way people learn. Students can now search for information instantly, watch lessons online, and get feedback much faster than before. So I think the classroom of the near future may feel less like a place where students just sit still and absorb information, and more like a place where they engage, respond, and explore. In a way, the classroom may start to look less like a stage and more like a workshop.
components.chinese: 原因是，科技其實已經在改變人們學習的方式。學生現在可以即時搜尋資料、在網上看課堂內容，也可以比以前更快得到回饋。所以我覺得，未來不久的課室可能不再只是讓學生安靜坐著、被動吸收資訊的地方，而會更像是一個讓他們參與、回應，以及探索的空間。某程度上，課室可能會愈來愈不像一個舞台，反而更像一個工作坊。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that technology is already changing the way
people learn. Students can now search for information instantly, watch lessons
online, and get feedback much faster than before. So I think the classroom of
the near future may feel less like a place where students just sit still and absorb
information, and more like a place where they engage, respond, and explore. In a
way, the classroom may start to look less like a stage and more like a workshop.
[原因是，科技其實已經在改變人們學習的方式。學生現在可以即時搜尋資料、在網上看課
堂內容，也可以比以前更快得到回饋。所以我覺得，未來不久的課室可能不再只是讓學生安
靜坐著、被動吸收資訊的地方，而會更像是一個讓他們參與、回應，以及探索的空間。某程
度上，課室可能會愈來愈不像一個舞台，反而更像一個工作坊。]

components.english: For example, instead of copying notes for most of the lesson, students may use tablets or laptops to do research, answer questions in real time, or work through tasks at their own pace. Teachers may spend less time repeating information and more time guiding, explaining, and helping students think more deeply.
components.chinese: 例如，學生未來可能不再用大部分上課時間去抄筆記，而是會用平板電腦或手提電腦做資料搜集、即時回答問題，或者按自己的節奏完成任務。老師也可能會減少重複講解資訊的時間，而把更多精力放在引導、說明，以及幫助學生更深入思考上。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, instead of copying notes for most of the lesson,
students may use tablets or laptops to do research, answer questions in real time,
or work through tasks at their own pace. Teachers may spend less time
repeating information and more time guiding, explaining, and helping students
think more deeply.
[例如，學生未來可能不再用大部分上課時間去抄筆記，而是會用平板電腦或手提電腦做資
料搜集、即時回答問題，或者按自己的節奏完成任務。老師也可能會減少重複講解資訊的時
間，而把更多精力放在引導、說明，以及幫助學生更深入思考上。]

components.english: So, overall, I think one major change will be that classrooms become more technology-supported and more actively engaging than they are now.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我覺得其中一個很大的改變，會是課室變得更依賴科技支援，也比現在更強調主動參與。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I think one major change will be that classrooms
become more technology-supported and more actively engaging than they are
now.
[所以，整體來說，我覺得其中一個很大的改變，會是課室變得更依賴科技支援，也比現在
更強調主動參與。]

components.english: That said, I also think the biggest change may not be the gadgets themselves, but the shift towards more personalised and skill-based learning.
components.chinese: 不過，我也覺得，最大的改變未必只是那些科技工具本身，而是課堂會慢慢轉向更個人化、也更著重能力培養的學習方式。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think the biggest change may not be the gadgets
themselves, but the shift towards more personalised and skill-based learning.
[不過，我也覺得，最大的改變未必只是那些科技工具本身，而是課堂會慢慢轉向更個人
化、也更著重能力培養的學習方式。]

components.english: In the future, schools may place less emphasis on rote learning alone and more emphasis on creativity, communication, teamwork, and problem-solving. After all, facts are now only a few clicks away, but judgment still has to be built the slow way. That is why I think classrooms may become more flexible, with students discussing ideas, doing projects, and learning how to apply knowledge rather than simply store it. In other words, education may start caring a little less about what students can recite and a little more about what they can do.
components.chinese: 未來，學校可能會減少單純背誦式學習的比重，而更重視創意、溝通、合作，以及解難能力。畢竟，資訊現在只要按幾下就能找到，但判斷力仍然要慢慢培養出來。正因如此，我覺得未來課室可能會更有彈性，學生會有更多機會討論想法、做專題，並學習如何應用知識，而不只是把知識存起來。換句話說，教育可能會愈來愈少在意學生能背出多少東西，而愈來愈在意他們真正能做甚麼。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: In the future, schools may place less emphasis on rote learning
alone and more emphasis on creativity, communication, teamwork, and
problem-solving. After all, facts are now only a few clicks away, but judgment
still has to be built the slow way. That is why I think classrooms may become
more flexible, with students discussing ideas, doing projects, and learning how
to apply knowledge rather than simply store it. In other words, education may
start caring a little less about what students can recite and a little more about
what they can do.
[未來，學校可能會減少單純背誦式學習的比重，而更重視創意、溝通、合作，以及解難能
力。畢竟，資訊現在只要按幾下就能找到，但判斷力仍然要慢慢培養出來。正因如此，我覺
得未來課室可能會更有彈性，學生會有更多機會討論想法、做專題，並學習如何應用知識，
而不只是把知識存起來。換句話說，教育可能會愈來愈少在意學生能背出多少東西，而愈來
愈在意他們真正能做甚麼。]

components.english: For instance, instead of every student doing exactly the same worksheet in exactly the same way, one group might create a presentation, another might solve a real-life problem, and another might work on a project together. The room may feel busier, noisier, and more alive, but that may actually mean more learning is taking place.
components.chinese: 例如，未來未必再是每一位學生都用完全相同的方式做同一張工作紙；一組學生可能會做簡報，另一組可能會解決一個真實情境中的問題，還有一組則會一起完成一個專題。那樣的課室也許會看起來更忙、更吵，也更有生命力，但那其實可能代表真正的學習正在發生。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, instead of every student doing exactly the same
worksheet in exactly the same way, one group might create a presentation,
another might solve a real-life problem, and another might work on a project
together. The room may feel busier, noisier, and more alive, but that may
actually mean more learning is taking place.
[例如，未來未必再是每一位學生都用完全相同的方式做同一張工作紙；一組學生可能會做
簡報，另一組可能會解決一個真實情境中的問題，還有一組則會一起完成一個專題。那樣的
課室也許會看起來更忙、更吵，也更有生命力，但那其實可能代表真正的學習正在發生。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the classrooms of the near future will probably become more digital, more flexible, and more focused on real understanding than mechanical learning. The tools may change quickly, of course, but I think the deeper goal will be the same: helping students become not just better test-takers, but better thinkers.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說未來不久的課室大概會變得更數碼化、更有彈性，也更重視真正的理解，而不是機械式學習。當然，工具本身可能會變得很快，但我覺得更深層的目標其實不會變：那就是幫助學生不只是成為更會考試的人，而是成為更會思考的人。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the classrooms of the near future will probably
become more digital, more flexible, and more focused on real understanding
than mechanical learning. The tools may change quickly, of course, but I think
the deeper goal will be the same: helping students become not just better
test-takers, but better thinkers.
[總結來說，我會說未來不久的課室大概會變得更數碼化、更有彈性，也更重視真正的理解
，而不是機械式學習。當然，工具本身可能會變得很快，但我覺得更深層的目標其實不會變
：那就是幫助學生不只是成為更會考試的人，而是成為更會思考的人。]

question.english: What advantages do universities bring to society?
question.chinese: 大學會為社會帶來甚麼好處？

source_lines.text: Topic: What advantages do universities bring to society?
大學會為社會帶來甚麼好處？

components.english: I think one major advantage is that universities help society develop skilled, knowledgeable, and capable people.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個很大的好處是，大學能幫助社會培養出有技能、有知識，而且有能力的人。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one major advantage is that universities help society develop
skilled, knowledgeable, and capable people.
[我認為，其中一個很大的好處是，大學能幫助社會培養出有技能、有知識，而且有能力的

components.english: Universities are not just places where people collect degrees; they are places where people are trained to think, analyse, and solve problems. A society needs doctors, teachers, engineers, researchers, lawyers, and many other professionals, and universities often play a central role in preparing them. In that sense, universities are a bit like the engine room of a modern society: a lot of the talent that keeps the system running is shaped there.
components.chinese: 大學不只是讓人拿學位的地方；它同時也是訓練人思考、分析，以及解決問題的地方。一個社會需要醫生、老師、工程師、研究人員、律師，以及很多其他專業人士，而大學往往正正就在培養這些人方面扮演核心角色。從這個角度來看，大學有點像現代社會的引擎室：很多維持整個系統運作的人才，都是在那裡被塑造出來的。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Universities are not just places where people collect degrees; they
are places where people are trained to think, analyse, and solve problems. A
society needs doctors, teachers, engineers, researchers, lawyers, and many other
professionals, and universities often play a central role in preparing them. In
that sense, universities are a bit like the engine room of a modern society: a lot
of the talent that keeps the system running is shaped there.
[大學不只是讓人拿學位的地方；它同時也是訓練人思考、分析，以及解決問題的地方。一
個社會需要醫生、老師、工程師、研究人員、律師，以及很多其他專業人士，而大學往往正正
就在培養這些人方面扮演核心角色。從這個角度來看，大學有點像現代社會的引擎室：很多
維持整個系統運作的人才，都是在那裡被塑造出來的。]

components.english: For example, if a country wants good hospitals, better schools, safer buildings, or stronger public services, it needs well-trained people behind them. Universities help provide that foundation by giving students both specialist knowledge and the discipline to use it properly.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個國家想擁有更好的醫院、更好的學校、更安全的建築，或者更強的公共服務，它就需要背後有一群受過良好訓練的人。大學正正能透過提供專門知識，以及讓學生學會如何恰當地運用它，來建立這個基礎。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a country wants good hospitals, better schools, safer
buildings, or stronger public services, it needs well-trained people behind them.
Universities help provide that foundation by giving students both specialist
knowledge and the discipline to use it properly.
[例如，如果一個國家想擁有更好的醫院、更好的學校、更安全的建築，或者更強的公共服
務，它就需要背後有一群受過良好訓練的人。大學正正能透過提供專門知識，以及讓學生學
會如何恰當地運用它，來建立這個基礎。]

components.english: So, one clear advantage of universities is that they strengthen society by producing the people who keep it functioning and moving forward.
components.chinese: 所以，大學其中一個很明顯的好處，就是它透過培養那些維持社會運作並推動社會向前走的人，去讓整個社會變得更強。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear advantage of universities is that they strengthen
society by producing the people who keep it functioning and moving forward.
[所以，大學其中一個很明顯的好處，就是它透過培養那些維持社會運作並推動社會向前走
的人，去讓整個社會變得更強。]

components.english: On top of that, universities also benefit society by generating ideas, research, and social progress.
components.chinese: 除此之外，大學也會透過帶來新觀念、研究成果，以及社會進步，去讓整個社會受益。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, universities also benefit society by generating ideas,
research, and social progress.
[除此之外，大學也會透過帶來新觀念、研究成果，以及社會進步，去讓整個社會受益。]

components.english: To be honest, universities do more than teach students; they also create knowledge. They are often places where new discoveries are made, old assumptions are questioned, and important public debates begin. A good university can widen society’s horizons. It can help people think more critically, respond to change more intelligently, and imagine solutions instead of just living with problems. In other words, universities do not simply pass knowledge on; they also push it further.
components.chinese: 老實說，大學做的不只是教學生；它同時也在創造知識。很多新發現、對舊有假設的質疑，以及重要公共討論的開始，往往都發生在大學裡。一所好的大學能夠拓闊整個社會的眼界。它能幫助人們更有批判思考能力、更聰明地回應變化，並學會想出解決方法，而不只是被動承受問題。換句話說，大學不只是把知識傳下去；它同時也會把知識推得更遠。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, universities do more than teach students; they also
create knowledge. They are often places where new discoveries are made, old
assumptions are questioned, and important public debates begin. A good
university can widen society’s horizons. It can help people think more critically,
respond to change more intelligently, and imagine solutions instead of just
living with problems. In other words, universities do not simply pass knowledge
on; they also push it further.
[老實說，大學做的不只是教學生；它同時也在創造知識。很多新發現、對舊有假設的質疑，
以及重要公共討論的開始，往往都發生在大學裡。一所好的大學能夠拓闊整個社會的眼界。
它能幫助人們更有批判思考能力、更聰明地回應變化，並學會想出解決方法，而不只是被動
承受問題。換句話說，大學不只是把知識傳下去；它同時也會把知識推得更遠。]

components.english: For instance, research from universities may lead to medical advances, cleaner technology, better policy ideas, or new business innovation. At the same time, universities often bring together people from different backgrounds, which can make society more open-minded and more willing to engage with different perspectives.
components.chinese: 例如，大學裡的研究可能會帶來醫學進步、更潔淨的科技、更好的政策構想，或者新的商業創新。與此同時，大學也常常把來自不同背景的人聚在一起，這能令整個社會變得更開放，也更願意理解不同觀點。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, research from universities may lead to medical
advances, cleaner technology, better policy ideas, or new business innovation. At
the same time, universities often bring together people from different
backgrounds, which can make society more open-minded and more willing to
engage with different perspectives.
[例如，大學裡的研究可能會帶來醫學進步、更潔淨的科技、更好的政策構想，或者新的商
業創新。與此同時，大學也常常把來自不同背景的人聚在一起，這能令整個社會變得更開放
，也更願意理解不同觀點。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say universities bring enormous advantages to society because they develop both people and ideas. They train minds, they widen horizons, and they help turn a society from somewhere that merely functions into somewhere that can actually grow.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說大學為社會帶來很大的好處，因為它同時培養人，也孕育想法。它訓練頭腦、拓闊眼界，並幫助一個社會從只是勉強運作的地方，變成一個真正能夠成長的地方。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say universities bring enormous advantages to
society because they develop both people and ideas. They train minds, they
widen horizons, and they help turn a society from somewhere that merely
functions into somewhere that can actually grow.
[總結來說，我會說大學為社會帶來很大的好處，因為它同時培養人，也孕育想法。它訓練
頭腦、拓闊眼界，並幫助一個社會從只是勉強運作的地方，變成一個真正能夠成長的地方。]

question.english: Which is more important, research or teaching?
question.chinese: 研究和教學，哪一樣比較重要？

source_lines.text: Topic: Which is more important, research or teaching?
研究和教學，哪一樣比較重要？

components.english: If I had to choose one, I would say teaching is slightly more important overall, because knowledge does not help society very much if it stays in the hands of only a few people.
components.chinese: 如果一定要我選一樣，我會說整體上教學是稍微更重要的一方，因為如果知識只停留在少數人手上，它其實很難真正幫助整個社會。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: If I had to choose one, I would say teaching is slightly more important
overall, because knowledge does not help society very much if it stays in the
hands of only a few people.
[如果一定要我選一樣，我會說整體上教學是稍微更重要的一方，因為如果知識只停留在少
數人手上，它其實很難真正幫助整個社會。]

components.english: The main reason is that teaching is what passes knowledge on, shapes young minds, and turns information into real understanding. A brilliant discovery may be valuable, of course, but if nobody can explain it clearly, teach it properly, or inspire the next generation to build on it, its impact remains limited. In that sense, teaching keeps knowledge alive. It is not just about giving facts; it is about lighting the torch and handing it on.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，教學負責把知識傳承下去、塑造年輕人的思維，並且把資訊轉化成真正的理解。當然，一項出色的研究成果本身很有價值，但如果沒有人能把它清楚解釋、好好教會別人，或者啟發下一代在此基礎上再向前走，那它的影響始終是有限的。從這個角度來看，教學正正是在讓知識活下去。它不只是把事實講出來，而是把火炬點亮，然後交到下一個人手上。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that teaching is what passes knowledge on,
shapes young minds, and turns information into real understanding. A brilliant
discovery may be valuable, of course, but if nobody can explain it clearly, teach it
properly, or inspire the next generation to build on it, its impact remains
limited. In that sense, teaching keeps knowledge alive. It is not just about giving
facts; it is about lighting the torch and handing it on.
[主要原因是，教學負責把知識傳承下去、塑造年輕人的思維，並且把資訊轉化成真正的理
解。當然，一項出色的研究成果本身很有價值，但如果沒有人能把它清楚解釋、好好教會別
人，或者啟發下一代在此基礎上再向前走，那它的影響始終是有限的。從這個角度來看，教
學正正是在讓知識活下去。它不只是把事實講出來，而是把火炬點亮，然後交到下一個人手

components.english: For example, a university professor may be highly knowledgeable, but if they cannot teach well, their students may leave the classroom confused rather than inspired. By contrast, a strong teacher can take complex ideas, break them down, and make them meaningful. That can shape not just one lesson, but a person’s whole future.
components.chinese: 例如，一位大學教授也許學問很深，但如果他教得不好，學生離開課室時可能只會感到困惑，而不是受到啟發。相反，一位出色的老師能把複雜的概念拆開、講清楚，並且讓它真正有意義。這種影響塑造的，往往不只是一堂課，而可能是一個人的整個未來。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a university professor may be highly knowledgeable,
but if they cannot teach well, their students may leave the classroom confused
rather than inspired. By contrast, a strong teacher can take complex ideas, break
them down, and make them meaningful. That can shape not just one lesson, but
a person’s whole future.
[例如，一位大學教授也許學問很深，但如果他教得不好，學生離開課室時可能只會感到困
惑，而不是受到啟發。相反，一位出色的老師能把複雜的概念拆開、講清楚，並且讓它真正
有意義。這種影響塑造的，往往不只是一堂課，而可能是一個人的整個未來。]

components.english: So, if I had to put it simply, I would say teaching is slightly more important, because it is the bridge between knowledge and people.
components.chinese: 所以，如果要我簡單地說，我會認為教學稍微更重要，因為它是知識和人之間的一道橋樑。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to put it simply, I would say teaching is slightly more
important, because it is the bridge between knowledge and people.
[所以，如果要我簡單地說，我會認為教學稍微更重要，因為它是知識和人之間的一道橋

components.english: That said, I do not think research can ever be treated as secondary in an absolute sense, because without research, teaching would eventually have nothing new to offer.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為研究可以在絕對意義上被視為次要，因為如果沒有研究，教學遲早也會沒有新的內容可教。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think research can ever be treated as secondary in
an absolute sense, because without research, teaching would eventually have
nothing new to offer.
[不過，我也不認為研究可以在絕對意義上被視為次要，因為如果沒有研究，教學遲早也會
沒有新的內容可教。]

components.english: To be honest, research is what pushes the frontier forward. It is how medicine improves, technology advances, and society learns things it did not know before. If people only taught existing knowledge and nobody questioned it, tested it, or expanded it, education would gradually become stale. In other words, teaching passes on the map, but research redraws it.
components.chinese: 老實說，研究正正是推動邊界向前的力量。醫學之所以進步、科技之所以發展、社會之所以能知道原本不知道的事情，很多都來自研究。如果人們只是不斷重複既有知識，卻沒有人去質疑它、驗證它，或者拓展它，那麼教育慢慢就會變得陳舊。換句話說，教學是在把地圖傳下去，而研究則是在重新畫地圖。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, research is what pushes the frontier forward. It
is how medicine improves, technology advances, and society learns things it did
not know before. If people only taught existing knowledge and nobody
questioned it, tested it, or expanded it, education would gradually become stale.
In other words, teaching passes on the map, but research redraws it.
[老實說，研究正正是推動邊界向前的力量。醫學之所以進步、科技之所以發展、社會之所以
能知道原本不知道的事情，很多都來自研究。如果人們只是不斷重複既有知識，卻沒有人去
質疑它、驗證它，或者拓展它，那麼教育慢慢就會變得陳舊。換句話說，教學是在把地圖傳
下去，而研究則是在重新畫地圖。]

components.english: For instance, a science teacher may explain modern medical treatments in class, but those treatments only exist because researchers spent years investigating diseases and testing solutions. The same is true in areas such as climate science, engineering, or psychology. Without research, teachers would sooner or later be passing down yesterday’s answers to tomorrow’s questions.
components.chinese: 例如，一位科學老師可以在課堂上講解現代醫療方法，但那些方法之所以存在，本身就是因為研究人員花了多年時間去研究疾病和測試解決方案。氣候科學、工程學，或者心理學等領域也是一樣。如果沒有研究，老師遲早就只是在把昨天的答案，傳給明天的問題。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a science teacher may explain modern medical
treatments in class, but those treatments only exist because researchers spent
years investigating diseases and testing solutions. The same is true in areas such
as climate science, engineering, or psychology. Without research, teachers would
sooner or later be passing down yesterday’s answers to tomorrow’s questions.
[例如，一位科學老師可以在課堂上講解現代醫療方法，但那些方法之所以存在，本身就是
因為研究人員花了多年時間去研究疾病和測試解決方案。氣候科學、工程學，或者心理學等
領域也是一樣。如果沒有研究，老師遲早就只是在把昨天的答案，傳給明天的問題。]

components.english: Overall, I would say teaching is slightly more important in terms of its direct human impact, but research is equally essential in the long run. The two are not rivals so much as partners: if research creates knowledge, teaching makes sure that knowledge actually reaches the world.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說若以對人的直接影響來看，教學是稍微更重要的一方；但從長遠來看，研究同樣不可或缺。兩者與其說是競爭對手，不如說是彼此合作的夥伴：如果研究創造知識，那麼教學就是確保知識真正抵達世界的那一方。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say teaching is slightly more important in
terms of its direct human impact, but research is equally essential in the long
run. The two are not rivals so much as partners: if research creates knowledge,
teaching makes sure that knowledge actually reaches the world.
[總結來說，我會說若以對人的直接影響來看，教學是稍微更重要的一方；但從長遠來看，研
究同樣不可或缺。兩者與其說是競爭對手，不如說是彼此合作的夥伴：如果研究創造知識，
那麼教學就是確保知識真正抵達世界的那一方。]

question.english: What makes a good university student?
question.chinese: 甚麼特質會讓一個人成為好的大學生？

source_lines.text: Topic: What makes a good university student?
甚麼特質會讓一個人成為好的大學生？

components.english: Personally, I think a good university student is not simply someone who gets high marks; it is someone who is curious, disciplined, and willing to take responsibility for their own learning.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為一個好的大學生，不只是考高分的人；更重要的是，他要有好奇心、有自律能力，並且願意為自己的學習負責。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think a good university student is not simply someone who
gets high marks; it is someone who is curious, disciplined, and willing to take
responsibility for their own learning.
[就我個人而言，我認為一個好的大學生，不只是考高分的人；更重要的是，他要有好奇心、
有自律能力，並且願意為自己的學習負責。]

components.english: The main reason is that university is very different from school. No one is going to hold your hand every step of the way, so students need to manage their time, keep up with readings, meet deadlines, and stay motivated even when things get tough. In that sense, a good university student is not just a sponge that absorbs information; they are someone who actively engages with it, questions it, and makes something of it.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，大學和學校很不一樣。沒有人會每一步都牽著你走，所以學生需要自己管理時間、跟上閱讀進度、趕好截止日期，並且在事情變得困難時，仍然能保持動力。從這個角度來說，一個好的大學生不只是被動吸收資訊的海綿；他更是會主動投入、提出疑問，並把所學真正轉化成自己能力的人。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that university is very different from school.
No one is going to hold your hand every step of the way, so students need to
manage their time, keep up with readings, meet deadlines, and stay motivated
even when things get tough. In that sense, a good university student is not just a
sponge that absorbs information; they are someone who actively engages with
it, questions it, and makes something of it.
[主要原因是，大學和學校很不一樣。沒有人會每一步都牽著你走，所以學生需要自己管理
時間、跟上閱讀進度、趕好截止日期，並且在事情變得困難時，仍然能保持動力。從這個角
度來說，一個好的大學生不只是被動吸收資訊的海綿；他更是會主動投入、提出疑問，並把
所學真正轉化成自己能力的人。]

components.english: For example, a strong university student usually does not wait until the night before an assignment to start working. They may plan ahead, take notes carefully, join discussions, and ask lecturers for clarification when they are confused. That kind of attitude often matters more than raw intelligence, because steady effort can carry people a long way.
components.chinese: 例如，一個表現好的大學生，通常不會等到交功課前一晚才臨急抱佛腳。他可能會提早規劃、仔細做筆記、參與討論，並且在不明白時主動向老師請教。這種態度很多時比單純的天資更重要，因為穩定而持續的努力，往往可以帶人走得很遠。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a strong university student usually does not wait until
the night before an assignment to start working. They may plan ahead, take
notes carefully, join discussions, and ask lecturers for clarification when they are
confused. That kind of attitude often matters more than raw intelligence,
because steady effort can carry people a long way.
[例如，一個表現好的大學生，通常不會等到交功課前一晚才臨急抱佛腳。他可能會提早規
劃、仔細做筆記、參與討論，並且在不明白時主動向老師請教。這種態度很多時比單純的天
資更重要，因為穩定而持續的努力，往往可以帶人走得很遠。]

components.english: So, to me, what makes a good university student is not just academic ability, but the habits and mindset that allow them to learn seriously and independently.
components.chinese: 所以，對我來說，讓一個人成為好大學生的，並不只是學術能力本身，而是那些能讓他認真而獨立地學習的習慣和思維方式。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, what makes a good university student is not just
academic ability, but the habits and mindset that allow them to learn seriously
and independently.
[所以，對我來說，讓一個人成為好大學生的，並不只是學術能力本身，而是那些能讓他認
真而獨立地學習的習慣和思維方式。]

components.english: On top of that, I think a good university student also needs maturity, because university is not only about gaining knowledge; it is also about growing as a person.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為一個好的大學生需要一定的成熟度，因為大學不只是學知識的地方，同時也是一個讓人學會成長的地方。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think a good university student also needs maturity,
because university is not only about gaining knowledge; it is also about growing
as a person.
[除此之外，我也認為一個好的大學生需要一定的成熟度，因為大學不只是學知識的地方，
同時也是一個讓人學會成長的地方。]

components.english: A good student should be open-minded enough to listen to different views, humble enough to accept feedback, and resilient enough to recover from setbacks. After all, university life is rarely a smooth ride. There will be pressure, disappointment, and the occasional reality check. What really sets a good student apart is not whether they never stumble, but whether they can get back on their feet and keep moving forward.
components.chinese: 一個好的學生，應該要有足夠的開放心態去聽不同觀點，有足夠的謙遜去接受回饋，也有足夠的韌性去面對挫折。畢竟，大學生活很少會是一帆風順的。當中一定會有壓力、失望，甚至偶爾會被現實狠狠提醒。真正令一個好學生與別人不同的，不是他從不跌倒，而是他跌倒之後能否重新站起來，繼續向前走。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A good student should be open-minded enough to listen to
different views, humble enough to accept feedback, and resilient enough to
recover from setbacks. After all, university life is rarely a smooth ride. There
will be pressure, disappointment, and the occasional reality check. What really
sets a good student apart is not whether they never stumble, but whether they
can get back on their feet and keep moving forward.
[一個好的學生，應該要有足夠的開放心態去聽不同觀點，有足夠的謙遜去接受回饋，也有
足夠的韌性去面對挫折。畢竟，大學生活很少會是一帆風順的。當中一定會有壓力、失望，
甚至偶爾會被現實狠狠提醒。真正令一個好學生與別人不同的，不是他從不跌倒，而是他跌
倒之後能否重新站起來，繼續向前走。]

components.english: For instance, if a student fails a test or struggles in a group project, a mature response would be to reflect on what went wrong, improve their approach, and try again, rather than blaming everyone else or giving up. That kind of resilience says a lot about their character.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一位學生考試失手，或者在小組作業中遇到困難，一個成熟的反應應該是反思問題出在哪裡、調整自己的做法，然後再試一次，而不是把責任全推給別人，或者乾脆放棄。這種韌性，其實很能反映一個人的品格。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a student fails a test or struggles in a group project,
a mature response would be to reflect on what went wrong, improve their
approach, and try again, rather than blaming everyone else or giving up. That
kind of resilience says a lot about their character.
[例如，如果一位學生考試失手，或者在小組作業中遇到困難，一個成熟的反應應該是反思
問題出在哪裡、調整自己的做法，然後再試一次，而不是把責任全推給別人，或者乾脆放
棄。這種韌性，其實很能反映一個人的品格。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say a good university student is someone who combines discipline with curiosity, and ability with character. In the end, the truly outstanding students are not simply the ones who know how to study, but the ones who know how to learn and how to grow.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說一個好的大學生，是能把自律和好奇心、能力和品格結合起來的人。說到底，真正優秀的大學生，不只是會讀書的人，而是那些既懂得學習，也懂得成長的人。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say a good university student is someone who
combines discipline with curiosity, and ability with character. In the end, the
truly outstanding students are not simply the ones who know how to study, but
the ones who know how to learn and how to grow.
[總結來說，我會說一個好的大學生，是能把自律和好奇心、能力和品格結合起來的人。說到
底，真正優秀的大學生，不只是會讀書的人，而是那些既懂得學習，也懂得成長的人。]

question.english: Is higher education too expensive in your country?
question.chinese: 在你國家，高等教育是不是太昂貴了？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is higher education too expensive in your country?
在你國家，高等教育是不是太昂貴了？

components.english: I would say yes, it can be quite expensive in Hong Kong, especially for ordinary families.
components.chinese: 我會說，是的，在香港，高等教育的確可以相當昂貴，特別是對一般家庭來說。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I would say yes, it can be quite expensive in Hong Kong, especially for
ordinary families.
[我會說，是的，在香港，高等教育的確可以相當昂貴，特別是對一般家庭來說。]

components.english: The main reason is that university is never just about the tuition fee. Once you add in transport, meals, study materials, and sometimes accommodation, the cost can rise very quickly. In a city like Hong Kong, where the cost of living is already high, higher education can feel less like a simple bill and more like a heavy load on the family’s shoulders.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，大學開支從來都不只是學費本身而已。一旦再加上交通、膳食、學習材料，甚至有時還包括住宿，整體成本就會很快升上去。在像香港這樣生活成本本來就高的地方，高等教育很多時候與其說是一張普通帳單，不如說是一副壓在家庭肩上的重擔。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that university is never just about the
tuition fee. Once you add in transport, meals, study materials, and sometimes
accommodation, the cost can rise very quickly. In a city like Hong Kong, where
the cost of living is already high, higher education can feel less like a simple bill
and more like a heavy load on the family’s shoulders.
[主要原因是，大學開支從來都不只是學費本身而已。一旦再加上交通、膳食、學習材料，甚
至有時還包括住宿，整體成本就會很快升上去。在像香港這樣生活成本本來就高的地方，高
等教育很多時候與其說是一張普通帳單，不如說是一副壓在家庭肩上的重擔。]

components.english: For example, a student may manage to get into university, but that does not mean the family can relax. They may still need to worry about daily expenses, and some students even take part-time jobs just to stay afloat. So sometimes the pressure does not come from one big payment, but from many smaller costs piling up bit by bit.
components.chinese: 例如，一個學生即使成功入讀大學，也不代表家庭就可以鬆一口氣。他們仍然可能要為日常開支煩惱，而有些學生甚至要做兼職，才能勉強撐住。所以，有時真正的壓力並不是來自一筆特別大的支出，而是來自很多細小開支一點一滴地堆上來。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a student may manage to get into university, but that
does not mean the family can relax. They may still need to worry about daily
expenses, and some students even take part-time jobs just to stay afloat. So
sometimes the pressure does not come from one big payment, but from many
smaller costs piling up bit by bit.
[例如，一個學生即使成功入讀大學，也不代表家庭就可以鬆一口氣。他們仍然可能要為日
常開支煩惱，而有些學生甚至要做兼職，才能勉強撐住。所以，有時真正的壓力並不是來自
一筆特別大的支出，而是來自很多細小開支一點一滴地堆上來。]

components.english: So yes, for many people in Hong Kong, higher education can feel expensive enough to be a real concern.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，對香港很多人來說，高等教育確實昂貴到足以成為一個很實在的憂慮。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, for many people in Hong Kong, higher education can feel
expensive enough to be a real concern.
[所以，是的，對香港很多人來說，高等教育確實昂貴到足以成為一個很實在的憂慮。]

components.english: That said, I would not say it is completely out of reach for everyone.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不會說它對每一個人來說都是完全遙不可及的。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would not say it is completely out of reach for everyone.
[不過，我也不會說它對每一個人來說都是完全遙不可及的。]

components.english: Some students can get publicly funded places or financial support, and that can make a big difference. Also, many families still see university as worth the sacrifice, because they believe it opens doors and gives young people a better shot at the future. In other words, the cost may bite, but people often swallow it because they see education as an investment rather than just an expense.
components.chinese: 有些學生可以拿到政府資助學位，或者獲得經濟支援，而這就能帶來很大的分別。另外，很多家庭仍然認為讀大學值得付出，因為他們相信那能替年輕人打開更多門，也讓他們對未來有更好的機會。換句話說，那個代價也許會令人感到吃力，但很多人仍然願意承受，因為他們把教育看成一種投資，而不只是單純的支出。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Some students can get publicly funded places or financial
support, and that can make a big difference. Also, many families still see
university as worth the sacrifice, because they believe it opens doors and gives
young people a better shot at the future. In other words, the cost may bite, but
people often swallow it because they see education as an investment rather than
just an expense.
[有些學生可以拿到政府資助學位，或者獲得經濟支援，而這就能帶來很大的分別。另外，
很多家庭仍然認為讀大學值得付出，因為他們相信那能替年輕人打開更多門，也讓他們對
未來有更好的機會。換句話說，那個代價也許會令人感到吃力，但很多人仍然願意承受，因
為他們把教育看成一種投資，而不只是單純的支出。]

components.english: For instance, one student may study in a publicly funded programme and live at home, so the situation feels manageable. Another may need to pay much more or live away from home, and for that person the burden can feel much heavier. So the experience is not the same across the board.
components.chinese: 例如，有些學生讀的是受資助課程，而且仍然和家人同住，那樣情況可能就比較可應付。但另一個學生如果要付更多費用，或者需要搬出去住，那份負擔對他來說就可能沉重得多。所以，每個人的情況其實並不一樣。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, one student may study in a publicly funded
programme and live at home, so the situation feels manageable. Another may
need to pay much more or live away from home, and for that person the burden
can feel much heavier. So the experience is not the same across the board.
[例如，有些學生讀的是受資助課程，而且仍然和家人同住，那樣情況可能就比較可應付。
但另一個學生如果要付更多費用，或者需要搬出去住，那份負擔對他來說就可能沉重得多。
所以，每個人的情況其實並不一樣。]

components.english: Overall, I would say higher education in Hong Kong is expensive enough to be a serious issue, but not so expensive that it shuts every door. The real question is not just how much it costs on paper, but whether the student has enough support to carry the cost without being crushed by it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港的高等教育昂貴到足以成為一個嚴肅問題，但也還沒有昂貴到把所有門都關上。真正的問題不只是紙面上要花多少錢，而是學生有沒有足夠的支援，讓自己在承受這筆開支的同時，不會被它壓垮。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say higher education in Hong Kong is expensive
enough to be a serious issue, but not so expensive that it shuts every door. The
real question is not just how much it costs on paper, but whether the student
has enough support to carry the cost without being crushed by it.
[總結來說，我會說香港的高等教育昂貴到足以成為一個嚴肅問題，但也還沒有昂貴到把所
有門都關上。真正的問題不只是紙面上要花多少錢，而是學生有沒有足夠的支援，讓自己在
承受這筆開支的同時，不會被它壓垮。]

question.english: Do you think students should be taken to school by their parents or go by themselves?
question.chinese: 你認為學生應該由父母送去上學，還是自己去比較好？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think students should be taken to school by their parents or go
by themselves?
你認為學生應該由父母送去上學，還是自己去比較好？

components.english: Personally, I think it depends on the student’s age, maturity, and the safety of the journey, but in general, older students should gradually learn to go by themselves.
components.chinese: 我個人認為，這要視乎學生的年齡、成熟程度，以及上學路線是否安全；不過一般來說，年紀較大的學生應該逐步學會自己上學。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think it depends on the student’s age, maturity, and the
safety of the journey, but in general, older students should gradually learn to go
by themselves.
[我個人認為，這要視乎學生的年齡、成熟程度，以及上學路線是否安全；不過一般來說，年
紀較大的學生應該逐步學會自己上學。]

components.english: The main reason is that going to school alone can teach independence, responsibility, and basic life skills. It helps students learn how to manage time, follow routines, and deal with the outside world instead of always having someone hold their hand. In that sense, the journey to school is not just about getting from A to B; it can also be part of growing up.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，自己上學可以培養獨立性、責任感，以及基本生活能力。這能幫助學生學會管理時間、遵守日常規律，以及面對外面的世界，而不是永遠都有人牽著他們走。從這個角度來看，上學的路程不只是由 A 點去到 B 點而已；它同時也可以是成長的一部分。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that going to school alone can teach
independence, responsibility, and basic life skills. It helps students learn how to
manage time, follow routines, and deal with the outside world instead of always
having someone hold their hand. In that sense, the journey to school is not just
about getting from A to B; it can also be part of growing up.
[主要原因是，自己上學可以培養獨立性、責任感，以及基本生活能力。這能幫助學生學會
管理時間、遵守日常規律，以及面對外面的世界，而不是永遠都有人牽著他們走。從這個角
度來看，上學的路程不只是由 A 點去到 B 點而已；它同時也可以是成長的一部分。]

components.english: For example, if a secondary school student lives reasonably close to school and the route is safe and familiar, going alone can build confidence. They learn to leave home on time, deal with transport, and take care of themselves little by little.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個中學生住得不算太遠，而上學路線又安全和熟悉，那麼自己上學其實能建立自信。他們會慢慢學會準時出門、處理交通問題，以及照顧自己。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a secondary school student lives reasonably close to
school and the route is safe and familiar, going alone can build confidence. They
learn to leave home on time, deal with transport, and take care of themselves
little by little.
[例如，如果一個中學生住得不算太遠，而上學路線又安全和熟悉，那麼自己上學其實能建
立自信。他們會慢慢學會準時出門、處理交通問題，以及照顧自己。]

components.english: So, in many cases, letting older students go by themselves is a good idea, because it helps them stand on their own two feet.
components.chinese: 所以，在很多情況下，讓年紀較大的學生自己上學其實是好事，因為那能幫助他們學會獨立。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in many cases, letting older students go by themselves is a
good idea, because it helps them stand on their own two feet.
[所以，在很多情況下，讓年紀較大的學生自己上學其實是好事，因為那能幫助他們學會獨

components.english: That said, I do not think all students should go to school alone, especially when they are still very young.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為所有學生都應該自己上學，特別是在他們還很年幼的時候。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think all students should go to school alone,
especially when they are still very young.
[不過，我並不認為所有學生都應該自己上學，特別是在他們還很年幼的時候。]

components.english: Young children usually need more protection and guidance. If the roads are busy, the school is far away, public transport is complicated, or the area feels unsafe, then parents taking them to school makes perfect sense. In some situations, safety has to come first. Independence matters, of course, but it should not be forced too early or thrown at a child before they are ready.
components.chinese: 年紀很小的孩子通常需要更多保護和引導。如果馬路很繁忙、學校距離很遠、公共交通很複雜，或者那個地區讓人覺得不夠安全，那麼由父母送他們上學就是完全合理的。在某些情況下，安全始終應該放在第一位。獨立固然重要，但不應該太早被強加，也不應該在孩子還未準備好之前就一下子丟給他。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Young children usually need more protection and guidance. If
the roads are busy, the school is far away, public transport is complicated, or the
area feels unsafe, then parents taking them to school makes perfect sense. In
some situations, safety has to come first. Independence matters, of course, but it
should not be forced too early or thrown at a child before they are ready.
[年紀很小的孩子通常需要更多保護和引導。如果馬路很繁忙、學校距離很遠、公共交通很
複雜，或者那個地區讓人覺得不夠安全，那麼由父母送他們上學就是完全合理的。在某些情
況下，安全始終應該放在第一位。獨立固然重要，但不應該太早被強加，也不應該在孩子還
未準備好之前就一下子丟給他。]

components.english: For instance, if a primary school child has to cross several busy roads or take more than one form of transport, most parents would probably feel more comfortable accompanying them. In that case, being careful is not overprotective; it is simply sensible.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個小學生上學途中要穿過幾條繁忙馬路，或者要轉乘多種交通工具，大多數父母大概都會覺得親自陪同比較安心。在這種情況下，小心一點並不算過度保護，而只是合理而已。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a primary school child has to cross several busy
roads or take more than one form of transport, most parents would probably
feel more comfortable accompanying them. In that case, being careful is not
overprotective; it is simply sensible.
[例如，如果一個小學生上學途中要穿過幾條繁忙馬路，或者要轉乘多種交通工具，大多數
父母大概都會覺得親自陪同比較安心。在這種情況下，小心一點並不算過度保護，而只是合
理而已。]

components.english: Overall, I would say younger children should usually be taken to school by their parents, while older students should gradually learn to go on their own. To me, the best approach is not all or nothing, but a gradual one that balances safety with independence.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說年紀較小的孩子通常應該由父母送去上學，而年紀較大的學生則應該慢慢學會自己去。對我來說，最好的做法不是非黑即白，而是循序漸進地在安全和獨立之間取得平衡。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say younger children should usually be taken to
school by their parents, while older students should gradually learn to go on
their own. To me, the best approach is not all or nothing, but a gradual one that
balances safety with independence.
[總結來說，我會說年紀較小的孩子通常應該由父母送去上學，而年紀較大的學生則應該慢
慢學會自己去。對我來說，最好的做法不是非黑即白，而是循序漸進地在安全和獨立之間取
得平衡。]

question.english: Should children rely on their parents or be independent?
question.chinese: 孩子應該依賴父母，還是應該獨立？

source_lines.text: Topic: Should children rely on their parents or be independent?
孩子應該依賴父母，還是應該獨立？

components.english: Personally, I think children should rely on their parents to some extent when they are young, because that is a natural and healthy part of growing up.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為孩子在年紀還小的時候，某程度上應該依賴父母，因為那本來就是成長中自然而健康的一部分。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think children should rely on their parents to some extent
when they are young, because that is a natural and healthy part of growing up.
[就我個人而言，我認為孩子在年紀還小的時候，某程度上應該依賴父母，因為那本來就是
成長中自然而健康的一部分。]

components.english: The main reason is that young children do not yet have the judgment, experience, or emotional stability to handle everything on their own. Parents are there to provide protection, guidance, and reassurance. In that sense, parental support is a bit like training wheels on a bicycle: it is not there to weaken the child, but to help them learn safely before they can balance by themselves. If children are pushed to be fully independent too early, they may end up feeling lost rather than strong.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，年紀小的孩子本來就還沒有足夠的判斷力、經驗，或者情緒穩定性去獨自處理所有事情。父母的角色，就是提供保護、引導，以及安全感。從這個角度來看，父母的支持有點像單車上的輔助輪：它的存在不是為了令孩子變弱，而是讓他們在真正能自己平衡之前，先安全地學會怎樣前進。如果太早把孩子推向完全獨立，他們最後感受到的可能不是力量，而是迷失。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that young children do not yet have the
judgment, experience, or emotional stability to handle everything on their own.
Parents are there to provide protection, guidance, and reassurance. In that sense,
parental support is a bit like training wheels on a bicycle: it is not there to
weaken the child, but to help them learn safely before they can balance by
themselves. If children are pushed to be fully independent too early, they may
end up feeling lost rather than strong.
[主要原因是，年紀小的孩子本來就還沒有足夠的判斷力、經驗，或者情緒穩定性去獨自處
理所有事情。父母的角色，就是提供保護、引導，以及安全感。從這個角度來看，父母的支持
有點像單車上的輔助輪：它的存在不是為了令孩子變弱，而是讓他們在真正能自己平衡之
前，先安全地學會怎樣前進。如果太早把孩子推向完全獨立，他們最後感受到的可能不是力
量，而是迷失。]

components.english: For example, if a primary school child is having trouble at school, whether with homework, friendships, or emotions, it makes perfect sense for them to turn to their parents first. At that stage, relying on parents is not a bad habit; it is part of learning how to deal with life.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一位小學生在學校裡遇到困難，不論是功課、人際關係，還是情緒問題，他先去找父母幫忙，其實是很合理的。在那個階段，依賴父母並不是壞習慣，而是學習如何面對生活的一部分。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a primary school child is having trouble at school,
whether with homework, friendships, or emotions, it makes perfect sense for
them to turn to their parents first. At that stage, relying on parents is not a bad
habit; it is part of learning how to deal with life.
[例如，如果一位小學生在學校裡遇到困難，不論是功課、人際關係，還是情緒問題，他先去
找父母幫忙，其實是很合理的。在那個階段，依賴父母並不是壞習慣，而是學習如何面對生
活的一部分。]

components.english: So, in early childhood, I would say relying on parents is both necessary and beneficial, because children need a secure base before they can move confidently into the world.
components.chinese: 所以，在童年早期，我會說依賴父母既有必要，也有好處，因為孩子在能夠有信心地走向外面的世界之前，本來就需要一個穩固而安全的起點。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in early childhood, I would say relying on parents is both
necessary and beneficial, because children need a secure base before they can
move confidently into the world.
[所以，在童年早期，我會說依賴父母既有必要，也有好處，因為孩子在能夠有信心地走向
外面的世界之前，本來就需要一個穩固而安全的起點。]

components.english: That said, as children grow older, I think independence becomes more and more important, and parents should gradually step back.
components.chinese: 不過，隨著孩子慢慢長大，我認為獨立就會變得越來越重要，而父母也應該逐步退後。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, as children grow older, I think independence becomes more
and more important, and parents should gradually step back.
[不過，隨著孩子慢慢長大，我認為獨立就會變得越來越重要，而父母也應該逐步退後。]

components.english: If children rely on their parents for too long or for too much, they may struggle to think for themselves, make decisions, or cope with setbacks. So the goal should not be dependence forever; it should be gradual independence. Good parents do not do everything for their children. Instead, they guide them, give them room to try, and let them make small mistakes while the stakes are still low. In other words, children need both roots and wings.
components.chinese: 如果孩子依賴父母太久，或者依賴得太多，他們將來可能會很難自己思考、自己作決定，或者面對挫折。所以，真正的目標不應該是永遠依賴，而應該是循序漸進地走向獨立。好的父母不會替孩子做完所有事情；相反，他們會引導孩子、給孩子空間去嘗試，並讓他們在後果還不算太嚴重的時候，先犯一些小錯。換句話說，孩子既需要根，也需要翅膀。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: If children rely on their parents for too long or for too much,
they may struggle to think for themselves, make decisions, or cope with
setbacks. So the goal should not be dependence forever; it should be gradual
independence. Good parents do not do everything for their children. Instead,
they guide them, give them room to try, and let them make small mistakes while
the stakes are still low. In other words, children need both roots and wings.
[如果孩子依賴父母太久，或者依賴得太多，他們將來可能會很難自己思考、自己作決定，
或者面對挫折。所以，真正的目標不應該是永遠依賴，而應該是循序漸進地走向獨立。好的
父母不會替孩子做完所有事情；相反，他們會引導孩子、給孩子空間去嘗試，並讓他們在後
果還不算太嚴重的時候，先犯一些小錯。換句話說，孩子既需要根，也需要翅膀。]

components.english: For instance, a teenager should gradually learn to manage their own schedule, travel to school, make sensible choices about study, and take responsibility for their own behaviour. Parents can still be there as a safety net, of course, but they should not be holding the steering wheel all the time.
components.chinese: 例如，一位青少年應該慢慢學會管理自己的時間表、自己上學、對學業作出合理選擇，以及對自己的行為負責。父母當然仍然可以作為一張安全網存在，但不應該一直把方向盤握在手裡。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a teenager should gradually learn to manage their own
schedule, travel to school, make sensible choices about study, and take
responsibility for their own behaviour. Parents can still be there as a safety net,
of course, but they should not be holding the steering wheel all the time.
[例如，一位青少年應該慢慢學會管理自己的時間表、自己上學、對學業作出合理選擇，以
及對自己的行為負責。父母當然仍然可以作為一張安全網存在，但不應該一直把方向盤握
在手裡。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say children should rely on their parents when they are young, but they should also be guided towards independence as they mature. The healthiest way to grow is neither endless dependence nor being pushed out too early, but learning to stand steadily with support until you can truly walk on your own.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，孩子在年幼時應該依賴父母，但隨著成熟，也應該被引導走向獨立。最好的成長方式不是永遠依附，也不是過早放手，而是讓孩子在被支持中學會站穩，最後真正能夠靠自己走路。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say children should rely on their parents when they
are young, but they should also be guided towards independence as they mature.
The healthiest way to grow is neither endless dependence nor being pushed out
too early, but learning to stand steadily with support until you can truly walk
on your own.
[總結來說，我會說，孩子在年幼時應該依賴父母，但隨著成熟，也應該被引導走向獨立。最
好的成長方式不是永遠依附，也不是過早放手，而是讓孩子在被支持中學會站穩，最後真正
能夠靠自己走路。]

question.english: How can children become more independent?
question.chinese: 孩子可以怎樣變得更獨立？

source_lines.text: Topic: How can children become more independent?
[孩子可以怎樣變得更獨立？]

components.english: I think children become more independent when adults gradually give them real responsibility instead of doing everything for them.
components.chinese: 我認為，當大人逐步把真正的責任交給孩子，而不是替他們包辦一切時，孩子就會變得更獨立。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think children become more independent when adults gradually give
them real responsibility instead of doing everything for them.
[我認為，當大人逐步把真正的責任交給孩子，而不是替他們包辦一切時，孩子就會變得更
獨立。]

components.english: Independence usually does not appear overnight. It grows step by step when children are trusted to do age-appropriate things on their own, such as getting dressed, packing their school bag, organising their homework, or helping with simple chores. If adults always step in too quickly, children may become too reliant on that help. In that sense, independence is a bit like a muscle: the more children use it, the stronger it becomes.
components.chinese: 獨立通常不會一夜之間出現。它往往是一點一滴長出來的，尤其當孩子被信任去自己完成一些符合年齡的事情時，例如自己穿衣、收拾書包、安排功課，或者幫忙做一些簡單家務。如果大人總是太快出手幫忙，孩子就可能會過度依賴那些幫助。從這個角度來說，獨立有點像肌肉：孩子越多使用它，它就會變得越強。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Independence usually does not appear overnight. It grows step
by step when children are trusted to do age-appropriate things on their own,
such as getting dressed, packing their school bag, organising their homework, or
helping with simple chores. If adults always step in too quickly, children may
become too reliant on that help. In that sense, independence is a bit like a
muscle: the more children use it, the stronger it becomes.
[獨立通常不會一夜之間出現。它往往是一點一滴長出來的，尤其當孩子被信任去自己完成
一些符合年齡的事情時，例如自己穿衣、收拾書包、安排功課，或者幫忙做一些簡單家務。
如果大人總是太快出手幫忙，孩子就可能會過度依賴那些幫助。從這個角度來說，獨立有點
像肌肉：孩子越多使用它，它就會變得越強。]

components.english: For example, if a child is expected to prepare their own school materials the night before or tidy up after playing, they slowly learn that daily life comes with responsibility. At first they may forget things or do them badly, but over time they usually get better and more confident.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子被要求前一晚先準備好自己的上學用品，或者玩完之後自己收拾東西，他就會慢慢明白，日常生活本來就伴隨着責任。一開始他也許會忘東忘西，或者做得不太好，但久而久之，他通常會愈做愈好，也愈來愈有信心。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child is expected to prepare their own school
materials the night before or tidy up after playing, they slowly learn that daily
life comes with responsibility. At first they may forget things or do them badly,
but over time they usually get better and more confident.
[例如，如果一個孩子被要求前一晚先準備好自己的上學用品，或者玩完之後自己收拾東西
，他就會慢慢明白，日常生活本來就伴隨着責任。一開始他也許會忘東忘西，或者做得不太
好，但久而久之，他通常會愈做愈好，也愈來愈有信心。]

components.english: So, one very effective way to build independence is to let children do more for themselves, even if it takes a little longer or looks a little messy at first.
components.chinese: 所以，要培養孩子的獨立性，其中一個很有效的方法，就是讓他們多為自己做一些事，即使一開始比較慢，或者看起來有點亂。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one very effective way to build independence is to let children
do more for themselves, even if it takes a little longer or looks a little messy at
first.
[所以，要培養孩子的獨立性，其中一個很有效的方法，就是讓他們多為自己做一些事，即
使一開始比較慢，或者看起來有點亂。]

components.english: On top of that, children become more independent when they are allowed to make small decisions and learn from mistakes, rather than being protected from every difficulty.
components.chinese: 除此之外，當孩子被允許作出一些小決定，並且從錯誤中學習，而不是被大人保護得太周全時，他們也會變得更獨立。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, children become more independent when they are
allowed to make small decisions and learn from mistakes, rather than being
protected from every difficulty.
[除此之外，當孩子被允許作出一些小決定，並且從錯誤中學習，而不是被大人保護得太周
全時，他們也會變得更獨立。]

components.english: If children never make choices for themselves, they may grow up waiting for someone else to tell them what to do. By contrast, when they are given some room to think, choose, and solve small problems, they begin to trust their own judgment. That said, this does not mean throwing them in at the deep end. They still need guidance and a safety net. The goal is to support them without smothering them.
components.chinese: 如果孩子從來沒有機會自己作選擇，他們長大後就可能總是在等別人告訴自己該怎樣做。相反，當他們有一些空間去思考、選擇，以及處理小問題時，他們就會開始相信自己的判斷。不過，這並不代表要一下子把他們丟進太深的水裡。他們仍然需要引導，也需要一張安全網。重點是在支持他們的同時，不要把他們包得太緊。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If children never make choices for themselves, they may grow
up waiting for someone else to tell them what to do. By contrast, when they are
given some room to think, choose, and solve small problems, they begin to trust
their own judgment. That said, this does not mean throwing them in at the
deep end. They still need guidance and a safety net. The goal is to support them
without smothering them.
[如果孩子從來沒有機會自己作選擇，他們長大後就可能總是在等別人告訴自己該怎樣做。
相反，當他們有一些空間去思考、選擇，以及處理小問題時，他們就會開始相信自己的判
斷。不過，這並不代表要一下子把他們丟進太深的水裡。他們仍然需要引導，也需要一張安
全網。重點是在支持他們的同時，不要把他們包得太緊。]

components.english: For instance, parents can let a child decide how to spend a small amount of pocket money, choose between two after-school activities, or work out how to fix a minor problem before stepping in. Even if the child makes the wrong choice now and then, that experience can teach far more than constant correction.
components.chinese: 例如，父母可以讓孩子自己決定怎樣使用一小筆零用錢、在兩個課外活動之間作選擇，或者先自己想辦法解決一個小問題，之後大人才介入。即使孩子偶爾作出錯誤決定，那種經驗很多時候也比大人不停糾正更能帶來學習。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, parents can let a child decide how to spend a small
amount of pocket money, choose between two after-school activities, or work
out how to fix a minor problem before stepping in. Even if the child makes the
wrong choice now and then, that experience can teach far more than constant
correction.
[例如，父母可以讓孩子自己決定怎樣使用一小筆零用錢、在兩個課外活動之間作選擇，或
者先自己想辦法解決一個小問題，之後大人才介入。即使孩子偶爾作出錯誤決定，那種經驗
很多時候也比大人不停糾正更能帶來學習。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say children become more independent when adults give them responsibility, decision-making space, and the chance to learn by doing. Independence is not built by keeping children in a bubble; it is built by letting them stretch their wings, little by little.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說孩子是在大人給予責任、給予作決定的空間，以及給予「邊做邊學」機會的過程中，慢慢變得獨立的。獨立不是靠把孩子放在泡泡裡培養出來的；它是在讓他們一點一滴張開翅膀的過程中建立起來的。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say children become more independent when adults
give them responsibility, decision-making space, and the chance to learn by
doing. Independence is not built by keeping children in a bubble; it is built by
letting them stretch their wings, little by little.
[總結來說，我會說孩子是在大人給予責任、給予作決定的空間，以及給予「邊做邊學」機會
的過程中，慢慢變得獨立的。獨立不是靠把孩子放在泡泡裡培養出來的；它是在讓他們一點
一滴張開翅膀的過程中建立起來的。]

question.english: What is the effect if parents interfere with children’s life too much?
question.chinese: 如果父母過度干涉孩子的生活，會有甚麼影響？

source_lines.text: Topic: What is the effect if parents interfere with children’s life too much?
如果父母過度干涉孩子的生活，會有甚麼影響？

components.english: I think if parents interfere too much, one of the biggest effects is that children may grow up less confident and less independent.
components.chinese: 我認為，如果父母干涉得太多，其中一個最大的影響，就是孩子長大後可能會變得較沒有自信，也較不獨立。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think if parents interfere too much, one of the biggest effects is that
children may grow up less confident and less independent.
[我認為，如果父母干涉得太多，其中一個最大的影響，就是孩子長大後可能會變得較沒有
自信，也較不獨立。]

components.english: The reason is that children learn by trying, choosing, and sometimes getting things wrong. If parents step in at every turn, children may start to doubt their own judgment and rely too heavily on someone else to make decisions for them. In a way, it is like never letting a child ride a bicycle without holding the seat: they may stay upright for a while, but they never really learn to balance on their own.
components.chinese: 原因是，孩子很多時都是透過嘗試、選擇，甚至犯錯去學習的。如果父母每一步都插手，孩子就可能開始懷疑自己的判斷，並且過度依賴別人替自己作決定。某程度上，這就像學踩單車時，永遠都有人抓住車尾不放：孩子也許暫時不會跌倒，但他始終學不會真正靠自己保持平衡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that children learn by trying, choosing, and
sometimes getting things wrong. If parents step in at every turn, children may
start to doubt their own judgment and rely too heavily on someone else to make
decisions for them. In a way, it is like never letting a child ride a bicycle without
holding the seat: they may stay upright for a while, but they never really learn
to balance on their own.
[原因是，孩子很多時都是透過嘗試、選擇，甚至犯錯去學習的。如果父母每一步都插手，孩
子就可能開始懷疑自己的判斷，並且過度依賴別人替自己作決定。某程度上，這就像學踩單
車時，永遠都有人抓住車尾不放：孩子也許暫時不會跌倒，但他始終學不會真正靠自己保持
平衡。]

components.english: For example, if parents always choose their child’s subjects, hobbies, friends, or daily schedule, that child may look obedient on the surface, but later on they may panic when they have to make even small decisions by themselves.
components.chinese: 例如，如果父母總是替孩子決定讀甚麼科目、參加甚麼興趣、和哪些朋友來往，甚至連日常時間表都安排好，那個孩子表面上也許看起來很聽話，但長遠來說，他可能連一些小決定都會感到慌張，因為他從來沒有真正自己作主過。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if parents always choose their child’s subjects, hobbies,
friends, or daily schedule, that child may look obedient on the surface, but later
on they may panic when they have to make even small decisions by themselves.
[例如，如果父母總是替孩子決定讀甚麼科目、參加甚麼興趣、和哪些朋友來往，甚至連日
常時間表都安排好，那個孩子表面上也許看起來很聽話，但長遠來說，他可能連一些小決定
都會感到慌張，因為他從來沒有真正自己作主過。]

components.english: So, in my view, too much interference can quietly weaken a child’s ability to stand on their own two feet.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，過度干涉會悄悄削弱孩子自己站穩陣腳的能力。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in my view, too much interference can quietly weaken a
child’s ability to stand on their own two feet.
[所以，在我看來，過度干涉會悄悄削弱孩子自己站穩陣腳的能力。]

components.english: Another effect is that it can damage the relationship between parents and children, because care can start to feel like control.
components.chinese: 另一個影響是，它也可能破壞親子關係，因為原本的關心很容易開始讓人感覺像控制。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea: Another effect is that it can damage the relationship between parents
and children, because care can start to feel like control.
[另一個影響是，它也可能破壞親子關係，因為原本的關心很容易開始讓人感覺像控制。]

components.english: When children feel watched, corrected, or managed all the time, they may begin to feel suffocated rather than supported. Love is important, of course, but if it becomes too heavy-handed, it can start to feel more like a cage than a shelter. At that point, children may stop opening up, hide things from their parents, or swing the other way and become rebellious just to protect a sense of self.
components.chinese: 當孩子覺得自己無時無刻都被盯著、被糾正、被安排時，他們很可能會覺得透不過氣，而不是感到被支持。愛當然很重要，但如果表達方式太強硬，它就可能開始更像一個籠子，而不是一個避風港。到了那個地步，孩子可能會不再願意向父母坦白、開始把事情藏起來，甚至走向另一個極端，變得反叛，只為了守住一點屬於自己的空間。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: When children feel watched, corrected, or managed all the time,
they may begin to feel suffocated rather than supported. Love is important, of
course, but if it becomes too heavy-handed, it can start to feel more like a cage
than a shelter. At that point, children may stop opening up, hide things from
their parents, or swing the other way and become rebellious just to protect a
sense of self.
[當孩子覺得自己無時無刻都被盯著、被糾正、被安排時，他們很可能會覺得透不過氣，而
不是感到被支持。愛當然很重要，但如果表達方式太強硬，它就可能開始更像一個籠子，而
不是一個避風港。到了那個地步，孩子可能會不再願意向父母坦白、開始把事情藏起來，甚
至走向另一個極端，變得反叛，只為了守住一點屬於自己的空間。]

components.english: For instance, if parents check every message, question every friendship, and comment on every choice, a teenager may stop sharing what they really think. They may smile on the surface, but emotionally, they start shutting the door.
components.chinese: 例如，如果父母查看每一則訊息、質疑每一段友誼，並且對每一個選擇都要加以評論，那麼一個青少年很可能會慢慢不再說出自己真正的想法。他表面上也許仍然在笑，但在情感上，其實已經開始把門關起來了。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if parents check every message, question every
friendship, and comment on every choice, a teenager may stop sharing what
they really think. They may smile on the surface, but emotionally, they start
shutting the door.
[例如，如果父母查看每一則訊息、質疑每一段友誼，並且對每一個選擇都要加以評論，那
麼一個青少年很可能會慢慢不再說出自己真正的想法。他表面上也許仍然在笑，但在情感
上，其實已經開始把門關起來了。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say too much parental interference can hurt both a child’s independence and the trust within the family. Parents should guide, of course, but not run the whole show. Children usually grow best when they feel supported, not suffocated.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，父母過度干涉既會傷害孩子的獨立性，也會影響家庭裡的信任。父母當然應該引導孩子，但不應該把整場人生都替他們操控好。孩子通常是在感到被支持，而不是被壓得透不過氣的情況下，成長得最好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say too much parental interference can hurt both a
child’s independence and the trust within the family. Parents should guide, of
course, but not run the whole show. Children usually grow best when they feel
supported, not suffocated.
[總結來說，我會說，父母過度干涉既會傷害孩子的獨立性，也會影響家庭裡的信任。父母
當然應該引導孩子，但不應該把整場人生都替他們操控好。孩子通常是在感到被支持，而不
是被壓得透不過氣的情況下，成長得最好。]

question.english: How does changing school affect children?
question.chinese: 轉校會怎樣影響孩子？

source_lines.text: Topic: How does changing school affect children?
轉校會怎樣影響孩子？

components.english: I think changing school can affect children quite deeply, especially at first, because it disrupts the world they have grown used to.
components.chinese: 我認為，轉校對孩子的影響其實可以很深，特別是在一開始，因為那會打亂他們原本早已習慣的世界。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think changing school can affect children quite deeply, especially at
first, because it disrupts the world they have grown used to.
[我認為，轉校對孩子的影響其實可以很深，特別是在一開始，因為那會打亂他們原本早已
習慣的世界。]

components.english: For many children, school is not just a place to study; it is where their routine, friendships, and sense of belonging are built. When that changes, they may feel unsettled, anxious, or even a little uprooted. In that sense, changing school can feel a bit like pulling up a young plant and moving it to new soil: it may survive perfectly well, but the adjustment period can still be delicate.
components.chinese: 對很多孩子來說，學校不只是一個讀書的地方；那裡同時也是他們建立日常節奏、友誼，以及歸屬感的地方。當這一切改變時，他們可能會感到不安、焦慮，甚至有點像失去根一樣。從這個角度來看，轉校有點像把一株還在成長的小植物連根拔起，再移到新的土壤裡：它最後當然可以活得很好，但適應期往往還是很脆弱。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: For many children, school is not just a place to study; it is where
their routine, friendships, and sense of belonging are built. When that changes,
they may feel unsettled, anxious, or even a little uprooted. In that sense,
changing school can feel a bit like pulling up a young plant and moving it to
new soil: it may survive perfectly well, but the adjustment period can still be
delicate.
[對很多孩子來說，學校不只是一個讀書的地方；那裡同時也是他們建立日常節奏、友誼，
以及歸屬感的地方。當這一切改變時，他們可能會感到不安、焦慮，甚至有點像失去根一
樣。從這個角度來看，轉校有點像把一株還在成長的小植物連根拔起，再移到新的土壤裡：
它最後當然可以活得很好，但適應期往往還是很脆弱。]

components.english: For example, if a child moves to a new school halfway through the year, they may have to deal with unfamiliar classmates, different teaching styles, and even a new social culture. At first, they may struggle to join friendship circles or feel nervous about standing out. That can knock their confidence for a while.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子在學年中途轉到新學校，他可能要面對陌生的同學、不一樣的教學方式，甚至一種新的社交文化。一開始，他可能很難融入朋友圈，也可能因為害怕自己太顯眼而感到緊張。這些都可能在一段時間內打擊他的自信。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child moves to a new school halfway through the
year, they may have to deal with unfamiliar classmates, different teaching styles,
and even a new social culture. At first, they may struggle to join friendship
circles or feel nervous about standing out. That can knock their confidence for a
while.
[例如，如果一個孩子在學年中途轉到新學校，他可能要面對陌生的同學、不一樣的教學方
式，甚至一種新的社交文化。一開始，他可能很難融入朋友圈，也可能因為害怕自己太顯眼
而感到緊張。這些都可能在一段時間內打擊他的自信。]

components.english: So, one clear effect of changing school is that it can create emotional and social stress, especially in the early stage.
components.chinese: 所以，轉校其中一個很明顯的影響，就是它可能會在最初階段帶來情緒和社交上的壓力。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear effect of changing school is that it can create
emotional and social stress, especially in the early stage.
[所以，轉校其中一個很明顯的影響，就是它可能會在最初階段帶來情緒和社交上的壓力。]

components.english: That said, changing school can also have positive effects, because it can help children become more adaptable, independent, and socially resilient.
components.chinese: 不過，轉校也可以帶來正面影響，因為它能幫助孩子變得更有適應力、更獨立，也更有社交上的韌性。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, changing school can also have positive effects, because it can
help children become more adaptable, independent, and socially resilient.
[不過，轉校也可以帶來正面影響，因為它能幫助孩子變得更有適應力、更獨立，也更有社
交上的韌性。]

components.english: If the child is supported well, a new school can become a fresh start rather than a setback. They may meet better friends, discover new interests, or leave behind an environment that did not suit them. In other words, the change may feel like a stumbling block at first, but it can later turn into a stepping stone. Sometimes children grow precisely because life asks them to stretch a little.
components.chinese: 如果孩子得到足夠支持，新學校就可能成為一個新的開始，而不是一個挫折。他們也許會遇到更適合自己的朋友、發掘新的興趣，或者離開一個本來就不太適合自己的環境。換句話說，那種改變一開始也許像一塊絆腳石，但後來卻可能變成一塊踏腳石。有時候，孩子正正就是因為人生要求他稍微伸展一下，才會慢慢成長。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: If the child is supported well, a new school can become a fresh
start rather than a setback. They may meet better friends, discover new
interests, or leave behind an environment that did not suit them. In other
words, the change may feel like a stumbling block at first, but it can later turn
into a stepping stone. Sometimes children grow precisely because life asks them
to stretch a little.
[如果孩子得到足夠支持，新學校就可能成為一個新的開始，而不是一個挫折。他們也許會
遇到更適合自己的朋友、發掘新的興趣，或者離開一個本來就不太適合自己的環境。換句話
說，那種改變一開始也許像一塊絆腳石，但後來卻可能變成一塊踏腳石。有時候，孩子正正
就是因為人生要求他稍微伸展一下，才會慢慢成長。]

components.english: For instance, a child who was quiet or unhappy in their old school may gradually open up in a new environment, join clubs, make closer friends, and become much more confident. A change of school can sometimes give a child the chance to rewrite the story they were telling themselves.
components.chinese: 例如，一個原本在舊學校裡很安靜，或者不太快樂的孩子，到了新環境之後，可能會慢慢變得更願意打開自己、參加活動、交到更親密的朋友，並且變得更有自信。有時候，轉校其實會給孩子一個機會，去重新寫自己原本正在經歷的故事。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a child who was quiet or unhappy in their old school
may gradually open up in a new environment, join clubs, make closer friends,
and become much more confident. A change of school can sometimes give a child
the chance to rewrite the story they were telling themselves.
[例如，一個原本在舊學校裡很安靜，或者不太快樂的孩子，到了新環境之後，可能會慢慢
變得更願意打開自己、參加活動、交到更親密的朋友，並且變得更有自信。有時候，轉校其
實會給孩子一個機會，去重新寫自己原本正在經歷的故事。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say changing school can be both challenging and beneficial. A lot depends on the child’s personality, the timing of the move, and the support they receive. With the right care, what begins as a disruption can end up becoming a period of real growth.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說轉校既可能帶來挑戰，也可能帶來好處。很多事情都取決於孩子的性格、轉校的時機，以及他得到多少支持。只要有合適的照顧和引導，一開始看似打亂生活的改變，最後也可能變成一段真正促進成長的時期。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say changing school can be both challenging and
beneficial. A lot depends on the child’s personality, the timing of the move, and
the support they receive. With the right care, what begins as a disruption can
end up becoming a period of real growth.
[總結來說，我會說轉校既可能帶來挑戰，也可能帶來好處。很多事情都取決於孩子的性
格、轉校的時機，以及他得到多少支持。只要有合適的照顧和引導，一開始看似打亂生活的
改變，最後也可能變成一段真正促進成長的時期。]

question.english: Do you think science is important?
question.chinese: 你認為科學重要嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think science is important?
你認為科學重要嗎？

components.english: Yes, absolutely, I think science is extremely important, because it shapes the way we live, think, and solve problems.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對重要。我認為科學非常重要，因為它會影響我們怎樣生活、怎樣思考，以及怎樣解決問題。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely, I think science is extremely important, because it shapes
the way we live, think, and solve problems.
