[是的，絕對重要。我認為科學非常重要，因為它會影響我們怎樣生活、怎樣思考，以及怎樣
解決問題。]

components.english: The main reason is that science is woven into everyday life, even when people do not notice it. It improves medicine, transport, communication, food production, and countless other parts of modern society. In that sense, science is not just something that happens in a laboratory; it is one of the engines that keeps the world moving forward. Without it, life would be not only less convenient, but also less safe and less advanced.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，科學其實深深融入日常生活之中，即使人們未必時時刻刻留意到它。它改善了醫療、交通、通訊、糧食生產，以及現代社會中數不清的其他部分。從這個角度來說，科學並不是只發生在實驗室裡的東西；它其實是推動世界向前走的其中一個引擎。如果沒有它，生活不但會沒那麼方便，也會沒那麼安全，甚至沒那麼進步。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that science is woven into everyday life, even
when people do not notice it. It improves medicine, transport, communication,
food production, and countless other parts of modern society. In that sense,
science is not just something that happens in a laboratory; it is one of the
engines that keeps the world moving forward. Without it, life would be not
only less convenient, but also less safe and less advanced.
[主要原因是，科學其實深深融入日常生活之中，即使人們未必時時刻刻留意到它。它改善
了醫療、交通、通訊、糧食生產，以及現代社會中數不清的其他部分。從這個角度來說，科學
並不是只發生在實驗室裡的東西；它其實是推動世界向前走的其中一個引擎。如果沒有它，
生活不但會沒那麼方便，也會沒那麼安全，甚至沒那麼進步。]

components.english: For example, vaccines, smartphones, clean water systems, and even simple household appliances all rely on scientific knowledge in one way or another. People may not think about science when they turn on a light or take medicine, but science is quietly doing the heavy lifting in the background.
components.chinese: 例如，疫苗、智能電話、食水淨化系統，甚至一些簡單的家居電器，其實都在某程度上依賴科學知識。人們在開燈或吃藥的時候，也許不會特別想到科學，但科學其實一直都在背後默默做最重要的支撐工作。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, vaccines, smartphones, clean water systems, and even
simple household appliances all rely on scientific knowledge in one way or
another. People may not think about science when they turn on a light or take
medicine, but science is quietly doing the heavy lifting in the background.
[例如，疫苗、智能電話、食水淨化系統，甚至一些簡單的家居電器，其實都在某程度上依賴
科學知識。人們在開燈或吃藥的時候，也許不會特別想到科學，但科學其實一直都在背後默
默做最重要的支撐工作。]

components.english: So yes, I would definitely say science is important, because it improves daily life in practical and often life-changing ways.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我肯定會說科學很重要，因為它以實際而且很多時足以改變人生的方式，改善了我們的日常生活。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would definitely say science is important, because it
improves daily life in practical and often life-changing ways.
[所以，是的，我肯定會說科學很重要，因為它以實際而且很多時足以改變人生的方式，改善
了我們的日常生活。]

components.english: That said, I do not think science alone is enough, because knowledge needs to be guided by ethics, judgment, and human values.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為單靠科學就已經足夠，因為知識本身仍然需要由倫理、判斷力，以及人類價值觀去引導。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think science alone is enough, because knowledge
needs to be guided by ethics, judgment, and human values.
[不過，我也不認為單靠科學就已經足夠，因為知識本身仍然需要由倫理、判斷力，以及人類
價值觀去引導。]

components.english: To be honest, science gives people power, but it does not automatically tell them how that power should be used. A discovery can become a blessing or a burden depending on the choices people make. In other words, science can light the path, but it cannot walk it for us. That is why societies also need wisdom, responsibility, and compassion alongside scientific progress.
components.chinese: 老實說，科學給人力量，但它並不會自動告訴人應該怎樣使用這種力量。一項發現究竟會成為祝福還是負擔，很多時取決於人作出甚麼選擇。換句話說，科學可以把前面的路照亮，但它不能代替我們走那條路。這也正是為甚麼社會除了需要科學進步之外，也同樣需要智慧、責任感，以及同理心。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, science gives people power, but it does not
automatically tell them how that power should be used. A discovery can become
a blessing or a burden depending on the choices people make. In other words,
science can light the path, but it cannot walk it for us. That is why societies also
need wisdom, responsibility, and compassion alongside scientific progress.
[老實說，科學給人力量，但它並不會自動告訴人應該怎樣使用這種力量。一項發現究竟會
成為祝福還是負擔，很多時取決於人作出甚麼選擇。換句話說，科學可以把前面的路照亮，
但它不能代替我們走那條路。這也正是為甚麼社會除了需要科學進步之外，也同樣需要智
慧、責任感，以及同理心。]

components.english: For instance, developments in artificial intelligence or genetic technology can bring enormous benefits, but they can also raise serious questions about fairness, privacy, or moral limits. So while science gives us the tools, people still need to decide how to use them responsibly.
components.chinese: 例如，人工智能或基因科技的發展，的確可以帶來巨大的好處，但同時也會引發一些關於公平、私隱，或者道德界線的嚴肅問題。所以，雖然科學把工具交到我們手上，但人仍然需要決定怎樣負責任地去使用它們。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, developments in artificial intelligence or genetic
technology can bring enormous benefits, but they can also raise serious
questions about fairness, privacy, or moral limits. So while science gives us the
tools, people still need to decide how to use them responsibly.
[例如，人工智能或基因科技的發展，的確可以帶來巨大的好處，但同時也會引發一些關於
公平、私隱，或者道德界線的嚴肅問題。所以，雖然科學把工具交到我們手上，但人仍然需
要決定怎樣負責任地去使用它們。]

components.english: Overall, I think science is hugely important, because it helps society understand the world and improve human life. However, its real value is greatest when it goes hand in hand with ethics and good judgment.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為科學非常重要，因為它幫助社會理解世界，也幫助改善人類生活。不過，它最有價值的時候，往往是它能和倫理觀以及良好判斷力並肩而行的時候。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I think science is hugely important, because it helps
society understand the world and improve human life. However, its real value is
greatest when it goes hand in hand with ethics and good judgment.
[總結來說，我認為科學非常重要，因為它幫助社會理解世界，也幫助改善人類生活。不過，
它最有價值的時候，往往是它能和倫理觀以及良好判斷力並肩而行的時候。]

question.english: Do you think science can change our society?
question.chinese: 你認為科學可以改變我們的社會嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think science can change our society?
你認為科學可以改變我們的社會嗎？

components.english: Yes, absolutely. I think science can transform society in profound ways, because it changes not only what we can do, but also how we live and what we expect from life.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對可以。我認為科學能夠深刻地改變社會，因為它不只是改變我們「能做甚麼」，也改變我們如何生活，以及我們對生活有甚麼期待。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely. I think science can transform society in profound ways,
because it changes not only what we can do, but also how we live and what we
expect from life.
[是的，絕對可以。我認為科學能夠深刻地改變社會，因為它不只是改變我們「能做甚麼」，
也改變我們如何生活，以及我們對生活有甚麼期待。]

components.english: The main reason is that scientific progress rarely stays inside the lab. Once a discovery proves useful, it tends to ripple outward into hospitals, schools, transport systems, workplaces, and ordinary homes. It can make people healthier, safer, and more connected, and it can also change the rhythm of daily life. In that sense, science is not just a body of knowledge; it is one of the quiet forces that keeps rewriting the rules of society.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，科學進步很少只停留在實驗室裡。一旦某項發現被證明有用，它往往就會像漣漪一樣向外擴散，進入醫院、學校、交通系統、工作場所，甚至一般人的家庭。它可以令人的生活更健康、更安全，也更彼此連結，同時還會改變日常生活的節奏。從這個角度來說，科學不只是一套知識；它更像是一股安靜但持續地改寫社會規則的力量。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that scientific progress rarely stays inside the
lab. Once a discovery proves useful, it tends to ripple outward into hospitals,
schools, transport systems, workplaces, and ordinary homes. It can make people
healthier, safer, and more connected, and it can also change the rhythm of daily
life. In that sense, science is not just a body of knowledge; it is one of the quiet
forces that keeps rewriting the rules of society.
[主要原因是，科學進步很少只停留在實驗室裡。一旦某項發現被證明有用，它往往就會像
漣漪一樣向外擴散，進入醫院、學校、交通系統、工作場所，甚至一般人的家庭。它可以令人
的生活更健康、更安全，也更彼此連結，同時還會改變日常生活的節奏。從這個角度來說，
科學不只是一套知識；它更像是一股安靜但持續地改寫社會規則的力量。]

components.english: For example, advances in medical science have allowed people to live longer and survive illnesses that once would have been devastating. In the same way, developments in communication and transport have made the world feel smaller and faster. A discovery may begin on a lab bench, but before long it can end up changing the texture of everyday life.
components.chinese: 例如，醫學上的進步讓人活得更長，也讓一些以前本來可能很嚴重、甚至致命的疾病變得可以控制。同樣地，通訊和交通方面的發展，也令整個世界感覺變得更小、更快。一項發現也許起初只是誕生在實驗桌上，但不久之後，它就可能改變日常生活的整體面貌。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, advances in medical science have allowed people to live
longer and survive illnesses that once would have been devastating. In the same
way, developments in communication and transport have made the world feel
smaller and faster. A discovery may begin on a lab bench, but before long it can
end up changing the texture of everyday life.
[例如，醫學上的進步讓人活得更長，也讓一些以前本來可能很嚴重、甚至致命的疾病變得
可以控制。同樣地，通訊和交通方面的發展，也令整個世界感覺變得更小、更快。一項發現
也許起初只是誕生在實驗桌上，但不久之後，它就可能改變日常生活的整體面貌。]

components.english: So yes, I would definitely say science can reshape society, because once it changes the conditions of life, society rarely remains the same.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我絕對會說科學可以重塑社會，因為一旦它改變了人類生活的條件，社會通常就不可能再維持原來的樣子。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would definitely say science can reshape society, because
once it changes the conditions of life, society rarely remains the same.
[所以，是的，我絕對會說科學可以重塑社會，因為一旦它改變了人類生活的條件，社會通
常就不可能再維持原來的樣子。]

components.english: That said, science does not automatically make society better, because everything depends on how human beings choose to use it.
components.chinese: 不過，科學並不會自動令社會變得更好，因為一切最終都取決於人類怎樣去使用它。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, science does not automatically make society better, because
everything depends on how human beings choose to use it.
[不過，科學並不會自動令社會變得更好，因為一切最終都取決於人類怎樣去使用它。]

components.english: The same scientific power that cures disease can also be used in harmful ways, and the same innovation that makes life easier can create new problems as well. Science is a powerful engine, but it still needs a steering wheel. If progress runs ahead without ethics, responsibility, or common sense, it can leave society moving faster but not necessarily moving wiser.
components.chinese: 能夠治病救人的科學力量，同樣也可能被用在有害的方向上；而那些令生活更方便的創新，也可能同時帶來新的問題。科學是一部很強大的引擎，但它仍然需要方向盤。如果進步跑得太快，而倫理、責任感和常識卻跟不上，那麼社會也許只是走得更快了，卻未必走得更明智。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The same scientific power that cures disease can also be used in
harmful ways, and the same innovation that makes life easier can create new
problems as well. Science is a powerful engine, but it still needs a steering wheel.
If progress runs ahead without ethics, responsibility, or common sense, it can
leave society moving faster but not necessarily moving wiser.
[能夠治病救人的科學力量，同樣也可能被用在有害的方向上；而那些令生活更方便的創新
，也可能同時帶來新的問題。科學是一部很強大的引擎，但它仍然需要方向盤。如果進步跑
得太快，而倫理、責任感和常識卻跟不上，那麼社會也許只是走得更快了，卻未必走得更明

components.english: For instance, nuclear science can provide energy and support modern life, but in different hands it can also be used destructively. That kind of example shows that science itself is not the whole story; human judgment is the other half of the equation.
components.chinese: 例如，核科學可以提供能源，支撐現代生活；但如果落在不同的人手中，它也可能被用在破壞性的方向上。這類例子正好說明：科學本身並不是故事的全部，人類的判斷才是另一半關鍵。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, nuclear science can provide energy and support
modern life, but in different hands it can also be used destructively. That kind
of example shows that science itself is not the whole story; human judgment is
the other half of the equation.
[例如，核科學可以提供能源，支撐現代生活；但如果落在不同的人手中，它也可能被用在
破壞性的方向上。這類例子正好說明：科學本身並不是故事的全部，人類的判斷才是另一半
關鍵。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say science can change society beyond question. In the end, the real issue is not whether science has the power to transform the world, but whether we have the wisdom to guide that power well.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說科學毫無疑問能夠改變社會。說到底，真正的問題不在於科學有沒有改變世界的力量，而在於我們是否有足夠的智慧，去好好引導這股力量。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say science can change society beyond question. In
the end, the real issue is not whether science has the power to transform the
world, but whether we have the wisdom to guide that power well.
[總結來說，我會說科學毫無疑問能夠改變社會。說到底，真正的問題不在於科學有沒有改
變世界的力量，而在於我們是否有足夠的智慧，去好好引導這股力量。]

question.english: Why do you think some inventions have not been successful in the marketplace?
question.chinese: 你認為為甚麼有些發明在市場上沒有成功？

source_lines.text: Why do you think some inventions have not been successful in the marketplace?
你認為為甚麼有些發明在市場上沒有成功？

components.english: I think one major reason is that being inventive is not enough; a product also has to fit real life.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個主要原因是，光有創意並不足夠；一件產品還必須真正融入現實生活。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one major reason is that being inventive is not enough; a product
also has to fit real life.
[我認為，其中一個主要原因是，光有創意並不足夠；一件產品還必須真正融入現實生活。]

components.english: Sometimes an invention sounds brilliant on paper, but once it meets ordinary life, the cracks begin to show. It may be too expensive, too complicated, or simply not useful enough for most people. If people do not feel a real need for it, they usually will not spend money on it. In other words, some inventions fail because they are really just a solution in search of a problem.
components.chinese: 有時候，一項發明在紙面上聽起來非常厲害，但一放進真實生活裡，問題就開始浮現。它可能太貴、太複雜，或者對大多數人來說根本不夠實用。如果人們感受不到它真正解決了甚麼需要，他們通常就不會願意花錢。換句話說，有些發明之所以失敗，是因為它們其實只是「先有解決方案，卻找不到真正的問題」。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Sometimes an invention sounds brilliant on paper, but once it
meets ordinary life, the cracks begin to show. It may be too expensive, too
complicated, or simply not useful enough for most people. If people do not feel a
real need for it, they usually will not spend money on it. In other words, some
inventions fail because they are really just a solution in search of a problem.
[有時候，一項發明在紙面上聽起來非常厲害，但一放進真實生活裡，問題就開始浮現。它
可能太貴、太複雜，或者對大多數人來說根本不夠實用。如果人們感受不到它真正解決了甚
麼需要，他們通常就不會願意花錢。換句話說，有些發明之所以失敗，是因為它們其實只是
「先有解決方案，卻找不到真正的問題」。]

components.english: For example, a company might invent a fancy kitchen gadget that promises to save time, but if it is hard to use, annoying to clean, and not much better than a simple knife or blender, people may lose interest very quickly. At first it may look clever, but after the novelty wears off, it just gathers dust.
components.chinese: 例如，一間公司可能發明出一件很花巧的廚房工具，聲稱可以節省時間；但如果它很難操作、清洗又麻煩，而且並沒有比一把普通刀或一部攪拌機好多少，人們很快就可能失去興趣。它一開始也許看起來很聰明，但新鮮感一過，最後往往只會在角落裡積塵。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a company might invent a fancy kitchen gadget that
promises to save time, but if it is hard to use, annoying to clean, and not much
better than a simple knife or blender, people may lose interest very quickly. At
first it may look clever, but after the novelty wears off, it just gathers dust.
[例如，一間公司可能發明出一件很花巧的廚房工具，聲稱可以節省時間；但如果它很難操
作、清洗又麻煩，而且並沒有比一把普通刀或一部攪拌機好多少，人們很快就可能失去興
趣。它一開始也許看起來很聰明，但新鮮感一過，最後往往只會在角落裡積塵。]

components.english: So, one clear reason some inventions fail is that they may be clever, but they never quite earn a place in people’s everyday lives.
components.chinese: 所以，有些發明之所以失敗，其中一個很明顯的原因就是：它們也許很聰明，但始終未能真正進入人們的日常生活。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear reason some inventions fail is that they may be
clever, but they never quite earn a place in people’s everyday lives.
[所以，有些發明之所以失敗，其中一個很明顯的原因就是：它們也許很聰明，但始終未能
真正進入人們的日常生活。]

components.english: Another reason is that timing and execution matter just as much as the invention itself.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，時機和執行方式，其實和發明本身一樣重要。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that timing and execution matter just as much as
the invention itself.
[另一個原因是，時機和執行方式，其實和發明本身一樣重要。]

components.english: A good idea can still flop if it arrives too early, is marketed badly, or asks people to change their habits too much too soon. People do not buy ideas in the abstract; they buy things that feel reliable, easy, and worth the price. If an invention feels awkward, unnecessary, or slightly ahead of its time, it may never really get off the ground.
components.chinese: 一個好點子即使本身不錯，也仍然可能因為出現得太早、宣傳做得不好，或者太快要求人們改變習慣，而最終失敗。人們不會為一個抽象概念付錢；他們真正會買的，是那些讓人覺得可靠、容易上手，而且物有所值的東西。如果一項發明給人的感覺很尷尬、很多餘，或者有點走在時代前面，它就可能始終無法真正起飛。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: A good idea can still flop if it arrives too early, is marketed
badly, or asks people to change their habits too much too soon. People do not
buy ideas in the abstract; they buy things that feel reliable, easy, and worth the
price. If an invention feels awkward, unnecessary, or slightly ahead of its time, it
may never really get off the ground.
[一個好點子即使本身不錯，也仍然可能因為出現得太早、宣傳做得不好，或者太快要求人
們改變習慣，而最終失敗。人們不會為一個抽象概念付錢；他們真正會買的，是那些讓人覺
得可靠、容易上手，而且物有所值的東西。如果一項發明給人的感覺很尷尬、很多餘，或者
有點走在時代前面，它就可能始終無法真正起飛。]

components.english: For instance, some wearable devices have looked futuristic and exciting at first, but if people feel self-conscious using them in public, or think the price is too high for what they actually do, enthusiasm can cool down very fast. A product can make a big splash at launch and still sink quietly afterwards.
components.chinese: 例如，有些穿戴式裝置一開始看起來很未來感，也很吸引，但如果人們覺得在公開場合使用它會有點尷尬，或者覺得它的功能根本不值那個價錢，那股熱情就很可能很快冷卻下來。一件產品也許剛推出時聲勢很大，但之後仍然可能靜悄悄地沉下去。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, some wearable devices have looked futuristic and
exciting at first, but if people feel self-conscious using them in public, or think
the price is too high for what they actually do, enthusiasm can cool down very
fast. A product can make a big splash at launch and still sink quietly afterwards.
[例如，有些穿戴式裝置一開始看起來很未來感，也很吸引，但如果人們覺得在公開場合使
用它會有點尷尬，或者覺得它的功能根本不值那個價錢，那股熱情就很可能很快冷卻下來。
一件產品也許剛推出時聲勢很大，但之後仍然可能靜悄悄地沉下去。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say some inventions are unsuccessful not because they lack brains, but because they lack fit — fit with people’s needs, habits, budgets, and timing. A smart invention may open the door, but the market decides whether anyone actually walks through it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，有些發明之所以不成功，不是因為它們不夠聰明，而是因為它們缺少一種「契合」——和人們的需要、習慣、預算，以及時機之間的契合。再聰明的發明也許能把門打開，但最後會不會真的有人走進來，還是要由市場決定。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say some inventions are unsuccessful not because
they lack brains, but because they lack fit — fit with people’s needs, habits,
budgets, and timing. A smart invention may open the door, but the market
decides whether anyone actually walks through it.
[總結來說，我會說，有些發明之所以不成功，不是因為它們不夠聰明，而是因為它們缺少
一種「契合」——和人們的需要、習慣、預算，以及時機之間的契合。再聰明的發明也許能把
門打開，但最後會不會真的有人走進來，還是要由市場決定。]

question.english: Do you think it’s good that new inventions are appearing so often?
question.chinese: 你認為新發明這麼頻繁地出現是一件好事嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think it’s good that new inventions are appearing so often?
[你認為新發明這麼頻繁地出現是一件好事嗎？]

components.english: Yes, overall, I do think it is a good thing, because new inventions often make life easier, safer, and more efficient.
components.chinese: 是的，整體來說，我的確認為這是一件好事，因為新發明往往會令生活變得更方便、更安全，也更有效率。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, overall, I do think it is a good thing, because new inventions often
make life easier, safer, and more efficient.
[是的，整體來說，我的確認為這是一件好事，因為新發明往往會令生活變得更方便、更安
全，也更有效率。]

components.english: The main reason is that invention is usually driven by need. People invent things to solve problems, save time, reduce effort, or improve quality of life. In that sense, new inventions are often signs that society is moving forward rather than standing still. Whether it is in transport, medicine, communication, or daily convenience, the right invention can take a real burden off people’s shoulders. It can turn what used to be slow, difficult, or frustrating into something far more manageable.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，發明通常都是由需要推動的。人們發明新事物，是為了解決問題、節省時間、減少勞力，或者提升生活質素。從這個角度來說，新發明很多時候正正代表社會在向前走，而不是停滯不前。無論是在交通、醫療、溝通，還是日常便利方面，一項合適的發明都真的可以替人減輕不少負擔。它能把那些本來緩慢、困難，或者令人煩躁的事情，變成更容易處理的事。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that invention is usually driven by need.
People invent things to solve problems, save time, reduce effort, or improve
quality of life. In that sense, new inventions are often signs that society is
moving forward rather than standing still. Whether it is in transport, medicine,
communication, or daily convenience, the right invention can take a real burden
off people’s shoulders. It can turn what used to be slow, difficult, or frustrating
into something far more manageable.
[主要原因是，發明通常都是由需要推動的。人們發明新事物，是為了解決問題、節省時間、
減少勞力，或者提升生活質素。從這個角度來說，新發明很多時候正正代表社會在向前走，
而不是停滯不前。無論是在交通、醫療、溝通，還是日常便利方面，一項合適的發明都真的
可以替人減輕不少負擔。它能把那些本來緩慢、困難，或者令人煩躁的事情，變成更容易處
理的事。]

components.english: For example, smartphones, online payment systems, and navigation apps have changed the rhythm of everyday life quite dramatically. People can communicate instantly, find their way more easily, and handle practical tasks in minutes rather than hours. In the same way, medical inventions can save lives, not just time, which shows how powerful innovation can be when it is used well.
components.chinese: 例如，智能手機、電子支付系統，以及導航應用程式，其實都相當明顯地改變了日常生活的節奏。人們可以即時溝通、更容易找到路，並且在幾分鐘內完成一些以往可能要花上很長時間的事情。同樣地，醫療方面的發明甚至不只是節省時間，而是可以直接救人一命，這也顯示了創新在被好好運用時可以有多大的力量。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, smartphones, online payment systems, and navigation
apps have changed the rhythm of everyday life quite dramatically. People can
communicate instantly, find their way more easily, and handle practical tasks in
minutes rather than hours. In the same way, medical inventions can save lives,
not just time, which shows how powerful innovation can be when it is used
well.
[例如，智能手機、電子支付系統，以及導航應用程式，其實都相當明顯地改變了日常生活
的節奏。人們可以即時溝通、更容易找到路，並且在幾分鐘內完成一些以往可能要花上很長
時間的事情。同樣地，醫療方面的發明甚至不只是節省時間，而是可以直接救人一命，這也
顯示了創新在被好好運用時可以有多大的力量。]

components.english: So yes, I would say frequent new inventions are generally a positive thing, because they help society solve problems and move with the times.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說新發明頻繁出現整體上是一件正面的事，因為它們能幫助社會解決問題，並且跟上時代。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say frequent new inventions are generally a
positive thing, because they help society solve problems and move with the
times.
[所以，是的，我會說新發明頻繁出現整體上是一件正面的事，因為它們能幫助社會解決問
題，並且跟上時代。]

components.english: That said, I do not think this is automatically good in every case, because not every invention improves life in a meaningful way.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為這在每一種情況下都一定是好事，因為並不是每一項新發明都真正有意義地改善生活。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think this is automatically good in every case,
because not every invention improves life in a meaningful way.
[不過，我也不認為這在每一種情況下都一定是好事，因為並不是每一項新發明都真正有意
義地改善生活。]

components.english: Sometimes inventions appear so quickly that people barely have time to think about the consequences. Some create waste, feed overconsumption, or make people overly dependent on convenience. Others are marketed as breakthroughs when, in reality, they are just old ideas in shinier packaging. In that sense, innovation can be a double-edged sword. It can open doors, but it can also create noise, pressure, and problems we then have to clean up afterwards.
components.chinese: 有時候，新發明出現得太快，人們甚至來不及思考它們帶來的後果。有些發明會製造浪費、助長過度消費，或者令人過分依賴便利。另一些則被包裝成重大突破，但實際上只是把舊東西換上一層更亮眼的外衣。從這個角度來說，創新也是一把雙刃劍。它可以打開新的門，但同時也可能帶來雜音、壓力，以及一些之後還得由我們自己收拾的問題。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Sometimes inventions appear so quickly that people barely
have time to think about the consequences. Some create waste, feed
overconsumption, or make people overly dependent on convenience. Others are
marketed as breakthroughs when, in reality, they are just old ideas in shinier
packaging. In that sense, innovation can be a double-edged sword. It can open
doors, but it can also create noise, pressure, and problems we then have to clean
up afterwards.
[有時候，新發明出現得太快，人們甚至來不及思考它們帶來的後果。有些發明會製造浪
費、助長過度消費，或者令人過分依賴便利。另一些則被包裝成重大突破，但實際上只是把
舊東西換上一層更亮眼的外衣。從這個角度來說，創新也是一把雙刃劍。它可以打開新的門
，但同時也可能帶來雜音、壓力，以及一些之後還得由我們自己收拾的問題。]

components.english: For instance, some digital inventions make life more convenient, but they can also shorten people’s attention span or make them feel glued to a screen. Likewise, some new products are replaced so quickly that perfectly usable ones get thrown aside, which is hardly a smart way to treat resources.
components.chinese: 例如，有些數碼發明的確令生活更方便，但同時也可能縮短人的專注力，或者令人整天像被黏在螢幕上一樣。同樣地，有些新產品更新得太快，結果很多明明仍然可以使用的東西卻很快被丟到一旁，這其實並不是一種明智對待資源的方式。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, some digital inventions make life more convenient, but
they can also shorten people’s attention span or make them feel glued to a
screen. Likewise, some new products are replaced so quickly that perfectly
usable ones get thrown aside, which is hardly a smart way to treat resources.
[例如，有些數碼發明的確令生活更方便，但同時也可能縮短人的專注力，或者令人整天像
被黏在螢幕上一樣。同樣地，有些新產品更新得太快，結果很多明明仍然可以使用的東西卻
很快被丟到一旁，這其實並不是一種明智對待資源的方式。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say it is good that new inventions are appearing so often, but only when they bring real value rather than change for the sake of change. The best inventions do not just dazzle people for a moment; they quietly make life better in the long run.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說新發明這麼頻繁地出現整體上是好事，但前提是它們帶來的是真正的價值，而不是為了改變而改變。最好的發明不是只讓人短暫地眼前一亮，而是能安安靜靜地在長遠之中把生活變得更好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say it is good that new inventions are appearing so
often, but only when they bring real value rather than change for the sake of
change. The best inventions do not just dazzle people for a moment; they quietly
make life better in the long run.
[總結來說，我會說新發明這麼頻繁地出現整體上是好事，但前提是它們帶來的是真正的價
值，而不是為了改變而改變。最好的發明不是只讓人短暫地眼前一亮，而是能安安靜靜地在
長遠之中把生活變得更好。]

question.english: Which area of science has been the most important in the last one hundred years?
question.chinese: 在過去一百年裡，你認為哪一個科學領域最重要？

source_lines.text: Topic: Which area of science has been the most important in the last one
hundred years?
在過去一百年裡，你認為哪一個科學領域最重要？

components.english: If I had to choose just one, I would probably say medical science.
components.chinese: 如果一定要我只選一個，我大概會說是醫學。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: If I had to choose just one, I would probably say medical science.
[如果一定要我只選一個，我大概會說是醫學。]

components.english: The main reason is that medical science touches human life at its most basic level. It is not just about making life easier or more efficient; it is about helping people stay alive, suffer less, and live with more dignity. Over the last century, medicine has changed the fate of millions of ordinary people, not in some abstract way, but in the most human way possible. When a field helps people live longer, recover faster, and fear illness less, it is very hard to put anything else above it.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，醫學觸碰的是人類生命最根本的層面。它不只是令生活更方便或更有效率，而是幫助人活下來、減少痛苦，並且活得更有尊嚴。在過去一百年裡，醫學改變了無數普通人的命運，而且那種改變不是抽象的，而是以最直接、最人性的方式發生的。當一個領域能幫助人活得更久、康復得更快，也減少對疾病的恐懼時，真的很難把其他東西放在它之上。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that medical science touches human life at its
most basic level. It is not just about making life easier or more efficient; it is
about helping people stay alive, suffer less, and live with more dignity. Over the
last century, medicine has changed the fate of millions of ordinary people, not in
some abstract way, but in the most human way possible. When a field helps
people live longer, recover faster, and fear illness less, it is very hard to put
anything else above it.
[主要原因是，醫學觸碰的是人類生命最根本的層面。它不只是令生活更方便或更有效率，
而是幫助人活下來、減少痛苦，並且活得更有尊嚴。在過去一百年裡，醫學改變了無數普通
人的命運，而且那種改變不是抽象的，而是以最直接、最人性的方式發生的。當一個領域能
幫助人活得更久、康復得更快，也減少對疾病的恐懼時，真的很難把其他東西放在它之上。]

components.english: For example, because of advances in medicine, illnesses that once sounded like a death sentence can now often be treated or controlled. Childbirth is safer, surgery is safer, and many infections that used to terrify people are no longer as frightening as they once were. That kind of progress does not just improve comfort; it changes the whole shape of life.
components.chinese: 例如，因為醫學進步，很多曾經聽起來幾乎等於判死刑的疾病，如今往往已經可以治療，或者至少受到控制。分娩更安全了，手術更安全了，而很多以前令人聞之色變的感染，也不再像從前那樣可怕。這種進步不只是提升舒適度而已；它其實改變了整個人生的形狀。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, because of advances in medicine, illnesses that once
sounded like a death sentence can now often be treated or controlled.
Childbirth is safer, surgery is safer, and many infections that used to terrify
people are no longer as frightening as they once were. That kind of progress
does not just improve comfort; it changes the whole shape of life.
[例如，因為醫學進步，很多曾經聽起來幾乎等於判死刑的疾病，如今往往已經可以治療，
或者至少受到控制。分娩更安全了，手術更安全了，而很多以前令人聞之色變的感染，也不
再像從前那樣可怕。這種進步不只是提升舒適度而已；它其實改變了整個人生的形狀。]

components.english: So, if I had to make a single choice, I would put medical science first, because few things matter more than protecting human life.
components.chinese: 所以，如果一定要我作出單一選擇，我會把醫學放在第一位，因為很少有甚麼事情比保護人的生命更重要。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to make a single choice, I would put medical science
first, because few things matter more than protecting human life.
[所以，如果一定要我作出單一選擇，我會把醫學放在第一位，因為很少有甚麼事情比保護
人的生命更重要。]

components.english: That said, I can understand why some people might choose fields like computer science or physics instead.
components.chinese: 不過，我也能理解為甚麼有些人會選擇像電腦科學或物理學這些領域。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I can understand why some people might choose fields like
computer science or physics instead.
[不過，我也能理解為甚麼有些人會選擇像電腦科學或物理學這些領域。]

components.english: After all, those areas have transformed the modern world in huge ways. They have changed how we communicate, work, travel, and learn. So I do not think there is only one respectable answer. Still, for me, medicine edges ahead, because computers may change the pace of life, but medicine often changes whether life can continue at all. One makes life faster; the other, very often, makes life possible.
components.chinese: 畢竟，這些領域也以非常巨大的方式改變了現代世界。它們改變了我們如何溝通、工作、出行，以及學習。所以，我並不認為這道題只有一個唯一值得尊重的答案。不過，對我來說，醫學仍然稍稍更勝一籌，因為電腦可以改變生活的節奏，但醫學很多時候改變的，卻是生命本身能否繼續下去。一個令生活變得更快，另一個則往往令生命變得可能。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: After all, those areas have transformed the modern world in
huge ways. They have changed how we communicate, work, travel, and learn. So
I do not think there is only one respectable answer. Still, for me, medicine edges
ahead, because computers may change the pace of life, but medicine often
changes whether life can continue at all. One makes life faster; the other, very
often, makes life possible.
[畢竟，這些領域也以非常巨大的方式改變了現代世界。它們改變了我們如何溝通、工作、
出行，以及學習。所以，我並不認為這道題只有一個唯一值得尊重的答案。不過，對我來說，
醫學仍然稍稍更勝一籌，因為電腦可以改變生活的節奏，但醫學很多時候改變的，卻是生命
本身能否繼續下去。一個令生活變得更快，另一個則往往令生命變得可能。]

components.english: For instance, if the internet goes down, life becomes inconvenient. But if effective treatment disappears, life can become frightening very quickly. That contrast, to me, says a lot. It shows why medicine sits so close to the heart of human life.
components.chinese: 例如，如果互聯網壞了，生活會變得很不方便；但如果有效的治療突然消失，生活就可能很快變得令人害怕。這種對比，在我看來已經說明了很多。它正好顯示出，為甚麼醫學會離人類生命的核心那麼近。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if the internet goes down, life becomes inconvenient.
But if effective treatment disappears, life can become frightening very quickly.
That contrast, to me, says a lot. It shows why medicine sits so close to the heart
of human life.
[例如，如果互聯網壞了，生活會變得很不方便；但如果有效的治療突然消失，生活就可能
很快變得令人害怕。這種對比，在我看來已經說明了很多。它正好顯示出，為甚麼醫學會離
人類生命的核心那麼近。]

components.english: Overall, I would say many branches of science have shaped the last hundred years, but if I had to name the most important, I would still choose medical science. Other fields have changed how we live, but medicine has done the most to help us live at all.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說很多科學分支都塑造了過去一百年；但如果一定要我說哪一個最重要，我仍然會選擇醫學。其他領域改變了我們怎樣生活，但醫學所做的，卻是在最大程度上幫助我們能夠活下去。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say many branches of science have shaped the
last hundred years, but if I had to name the most important, I would still choose
medical science. Other fields have changed how we live, but medicine has done
the most to help us live at all.

我們能夠活下去。]

question.english: Do you think there will be any negative effects resulting from future technology?
question.chinese: 你認為未來科技會帶來任何負面影響嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think there will be any negative effects resulting from
future technology?
你認為未來科技會帶來任何負面影響嗎？

components.english: Yes, definitely. I think future technology will bring enormous benefits, but it will also come with some real downsides.
components.chinese: 是的，肯定會。我認為未來科技當然會帶來巨大的好處，但同時也一定會伴隨一些真正的負面影響。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, definitely. I think future technology will bring enormous
benefits, but it will also come with some real downsides.
[是的，肯定會。我認為未來科技當然會帶來巨大的好處，但同時也一定會伴隨一些真正的
負面影響。]

components.english: To me, that is almost unavoidable, because every powerful tool casts a shadow. The more technology can do for us, the easier it becomes for people to depend on it too heavily. If machines begin to think, organise, and decide everything for us, people may gradually stop exercising their own judgment. In that sense, future technology may save time, but it could also make some people mentally lazier, less patient, and less present in their own lives.
components.chinese: 對我來說，這幾乎是無可避免的，因為每一種強大的工具背後都會投下一道影子。科技替我們做得越多，人就越容易過度依賴它。如果機器開始替我們思考、安排，甚至決定一切，人們就可能慢慢不再鍛鍊自己的判斷力。從這個角度來看，未來科技也許能節省時間，但同時也可能令一些人變得更懶於思考、更沒有耐性，也更難真正活在自己的生活裡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: To me, that is almost unavoidable, because every
powerful tool casts a shadow. The more technology can do for us, the
easier it becomes for people to depend on it too heavily. If machines
begin to think, organise, and decide everything for us, people may
gradually stop exercising their own judgment. In that sense, future
technology may save time, but it could also make some people
mentally lazier, less patient, and less present in their own lives.
[對我來說，這幾乎是無可避免的，因為每一種強大的工具背後都會投下一道影子。科技替
我們做得越多，人就越容易過度依賴它。如果機器開始替我們思考、安排，甚至決定一切，
人們就可能慢慢不再鍛鍊自己的判斷力。從這個角度來看，未來科技也許能節省時間，但同
時也可能令一些人變得更懶於思考、更沒有耐性，也更難真正活在自己的生活裡。]

components.english: For example, if future technology becomes so advanced that people rely on it for every small task, from writing messages to making daily decisions, they may begin to feel helpless without it. It would be a bit like having a machine hold your hand all the time: comfortable at first, but weakening in the long run.
components.chinese: 例如，如果未來科技先進到人們連寫訊息、作日常決定這些小事都完全依賴它，那麼一旦沒有它，他們就可能變得手足無措。這有點像一直有一台機器替你扶著手走路：一開始的確很舒服，但長遠來說，反而會把人本身的能力削弱掉。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if future technology becomes so advanced
that people rely on it for every small task, from writing messages to
making daily decisions, they may begin to feel helpless without it. It
would be a bit like having a machine hold your hand all the time:
comfortable at first, but weakening in the long run.
[例如，如果未來科技先進到人們連寫訊息、作日常決定這些小事都完全依賴它，那麼一旦
沒有它，他們就可能變得手足無措。這有點像一直有一台機器替你扶著手走路：一開始的確
很舒服，但長遠來說，反而會把人本身的能力削弱掉。]

components.english: So yes, one major negative effect may be overdependence, because convenience can quietly become a crutch.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，其中一個很大的負面影響可能就是過度依賴，因為便利很容易在不知不覺間變成一根拐杖。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, one major negative effect may be
overdependence, because convenience can quietly become a crutch.
[所以，是的，其中一個很大的負面影響可能就是過度依賴，因為便利很容易在不知不覺間
變成一根拐杖。]

components.english: On top of that, I think future technology could also create problems in areas like employment, privacy, and inequality.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也覺得未來科技很可能會在就業、私隱，以及不平等這些方面帶來問題。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think future technology could also create
problems in areas like employment, privacy, and inequality.
[除此之外，我也覺得未來科技很可能會在就業、私隱，以及不平等這些方面帶來問題。]

components.english: As technology becomes more advanced, some jobs may disappear or shrink, especially routine ones. At the same time, companies and systems may collect more and more personal data, which can make people feel watched rather than served. There is also the risk that the benefits of technology will not be shared equally. Some people may ride the wave, while others are left struggling to keep their heads above water. In other words, progress can lift society, but it can also widen the gap if it is not handled wisely.
components.chinese: 隨著科技愈來愈先進，有些工作可能會消失，或者被大幅壓縮，尤其是那些重複性較高的工作。與此同時，企業和系統也可能收集愈來愈多個人資料，令人感覺自己不是被服務，而是被監視。除此之外，科技帶來的好處也未必會被平均分享。有些人也許能順著浪潮往上走，但另一些人卻可能連浮在水面上都很吃力。換句話說，進步當然可以把整個社會推高，但如果處理得不好，它同時也可能把差距拉得更大。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: As technology becomes more advanced, some jobs
may disappear or shrink, especially routine ones. At the same time,
companies and systems may collect more and more personal data,
which can make people feel watched rather than served. There is also
the risk that the benefits of technology will not be shared equally.
Some people may ride the wave, while others are left struggling to
keep their heads above water. In other words, progress can lift
society, but it can also widen the gap if it is not handled wisely.
[隨著科技愈來愈先進，有些工作可能會消失，或者被大幅壓縮，尤其是那些重複性較高的
工作。與此同時，企業和系統也可能收集愈來愈多個人資料，令人感覺自己不是被服務，而
是被監視。除此之外，科技帶來的好處也未必會被平均分享。有些人也許能順著浪潮往上走
，但另一些人卻可能連浮在水面上都很吃力。換句話說，進步當然可以把整個社會推高，但
如果處理得不好，它同時也可能把差距拉得更大。]

components.english: For instance, if artificial intelligence can do a lot of office work more cheaply and more quickly, some workers may suddenly find that their skills are no longer enough. In the same way, if smart devices know where people go, what they buy, and what they say, life may become more efficient on the surface but less private underneath.
components.chinese: 例如，如果人工智能能以更低成本、更快速度完成大量文職工作，那麼一些員工可能會突然發現，自己原本的技能已經不再足夠。同樣地，如果智能設備知道人們去了哪裡、買了甚麼、說了甚麼，那麼生活表面上看起來也許更有效率，但底下卻可能變得更沒有私隱。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if artificial intelligence can do a lot of office
work more cheaply and more quickly, some workers may suddenly
find that their skills are no longer enough. In the same way, if smart
devices know where people go, what they buy, and what they say, life
may become more efficient on the surface but less private
underneath.
[例如，如果人工智能能以更低成本、更快速度完成大量文職工作，那麼一些員工可能會突
然發現，自己原本的技能已經不再足夠。同樣地，如果智能設備知道人們去了哪裡、買了甚
麼、說了甚麼，那麼生活表面上看起來也許更有效率，但底下卻可能變得更沒有私隱。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say future technology will almost certainly bring some negative effects, even though it will also improve life in many ways. The real question is not whether technology will change the world, because it will; it is whether we will be wise enough to stay in control of the change instead of being carried away by it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說未來科技幾乎肯定會帶來一些負面影響，雖然它同時也會在很多方面改善生活。真正的問題不是科技會不會改變世界，因為它一定會；而是我們是否有足夠的智慧，在改變之中仍然保持主導，而不是反過來被它牽著走。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say future technology will almost
certainly bring some negative effects, even though it will also improve
life in many ways. The real question is not whether technology will
change the world, because it will; it is whether we will be wise enough
to stay in control of the change instead of being carried away by it.
[總結來說，我會說未來科技幾乎肯定會帶來一些負面影響，雖然它同時也會在很多方面改
善生活。真正的問題不是科技會不會改變世界，因為它一定會；而是我們是否有足夠的智慧
，在改變之中仍然保持主導，而不是反過來被它牽著走。]

question.english: Is shopping a popular activity in your country?
question.chinese: 你認為在你的國家，購物是一項受歡迎的活動嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is shopping a popular activity in your country?
你認為在你的國家，購物是一項受歡迎的活動嗎？

components.english: Yes, very much so. In Hong Kong, shopping is definitely a popular activity for many people.
components.chinese: 是的，非常受歡迎。在香港，購物的確是很多人都喜歡的一項活動。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, very much so. In Hong Kong, shopping is definitely a
popular activity for many people.
[是的，非常受歡迎。在香港，購物的確是很多人都喜歡的一項活動。]

components.english: One reason is that shopping in Hong Kong is not just about buying things; for many people, it is also a way to relax, socialise, and enjoy the rhythm of the city. Because the city is full of malls, markets, shops, and now online platforms as well, shopping is woven quite naturally into everyday life. In that sense, it is not only a practical activity, but also a kind of leisure.
components.chinese: 其中一個原因是，在香港，購物不只是為了買東西；對很多人來說，它同時也是一種放鬆、社交，以及感受城市節奏的方式。由於這個城市到處都有商場、市集、店舖，而現在也有很多網上平台，所以購物很自然地融入了日常生活。從這個角度來看，它不只是一種實際活動，同時也是一種消閒。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: One reason is that shopping in Hong Kong is not just
about buying things; for many people, it is also a way to relax,
socialise, and enjoy the rhythm of the city. Because the city is full of
malls, markets, shops, and now online platforms as well, shopping is
woven quite naturally into everyday life. In that sense, it is not only a
practical activity, but also a kind of leisure.

，同時也是一種消閒。]

components.english: For example, at weekends, it is very common to see families in shopping centres, young people browsing clothes, cosmetics, or gadgets, and friends meeting up to shop and have a meal together. Even people who are not planning to buy much often still enjoy walking around and seeing what is new.
components.chinese: 例如，在週末，很常見到一家大小去商場、年輕人看衣服、化妝品或電子產品，而朋友之間也常常會約出來一起逛街和吃飯。即使有些人本來不打算買很多東西，他們很多時候也仍然享受逛一逛，看看有甚麼新鮮事物。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, at weekends, it is very common to see
families in shopping centres, young people browsing clothes,
cosmetics, or gadgets, and friends meeting up to shop and have a
meal together. Even people who are not planning to buy much often
still enjoy walking around and seeing what is new.

components.english: So yes, I would say shopping is popular, because in Hong Kong it often feels like part of both daily life and social life.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說購物是很受歡迎的，因為在香港，它很多時候既像日常生活的一部分，也像社交生活的一部分。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say shopping is popular, because in
Hong Kong it often feels like part of both daily life and social life.
[所以，是的，我會說購物是很受歡迎的，因為在香港，它很多時候既像日常生活的一部分，
也像社交生活的一部分。]

components.english: That said, not everyone enjoys shopping in the same way anymore, because the habit has changed with time.
components.chinese: 不過，現在並不是每個人都以同樣方式享受購物了，因為這種習慣其實已經隨時間慢慢改變。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, not everyone enjoys shopping in the same way
anymore, because the habit has changed with time.
[不過，現在並不是每個人都以同樣方式享受購物了，因為這種習慣其實已經隨時間慢慢改

components.english: To be honest, rising prices, busy schedules, and online shopping have changed the picture a lot. Some people still enjoy the buzz of physical shops, whereas others would rather compare prices online and have things delivered to their door. In other words, shopping is still popular, but it has become more flexible and more mixed. The habit has not disappeared; it has simply changed its clothes.
components.chinese: 老實說，物價上升、生活忙碌，以及網上購物的普及，都令整個情況改變了不少。有些人仍然很喜歡實體店那種熱鬧氣氛，但也有些人寧願在網上比價，然後直接送貨上門。換句話說，購物仍然受歡迎，只不過它變得更有彈性，也更混合化了。這個習慣並沒有消失；它只是換了一身衣服而已。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, rising prices, busy schedules, and
online shopping have changed the picture a lot. Some people still
enjoy the buzz of physical shops, whereas others would rather
compare prices online and have things delivered to their door. In
other words, shopping is still popular, but it has become more flexible
and more mixed. The habit has not disappeared; it has simply
changed its clothes.

然很喜歡實體店那種熱鬧氣氛，但也有些人寧願在網上比價，然後直接送貨上門。換句話說
，購物仍然受歡迎，只不過它變得更有彈性，也更混合化了。這個習慣並沒有消失；它只是
換了一身衣服而已。]

components.english: For instance, someone may buy daily necessities online during the week, but still go to a mall at the weekend for clothes, shoes, or simply the experience of being out and about. So the popularity is still there, but it now lives both on the street and on the screen.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人平日可能會在網上買日用品，但到了週末，仍然會去商場買衣服、鞋子，或者單純享受外出走走的感覺。所以，那份受歡迎的程度其實還在，只不過它現在同時存在於街上，也存在於螢幕裡。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone may buy daily necessities online
during the week, but still go to a mall at the weekend for clothes,
shoes, or simply the experience of being out and about. So the
popularity is still there, but it now lives both on the street and on the
screen.
[例如，一個人平日可能會在網上買日用品，但到了週末，仍然會去商場買衣服、鞋子，或者
單純享受外出走走的感覺。所以，那份受歡迎的程度其實還在，只不過它現在同時存在於街
上，也存在於螢幕裡。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say shopping is still a very popular activity in Hong Kong, although the way people shop is becoming more diverse. It is no longer just about carrying bags home; quite often, it is also about convenience, experience, and the small pleasure of choosing for yourself.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說購物在香港仍然是一項很受歡迎的活動，只不過人們購物的方式正變得更多元。它不再只是把購物袋帶回家那麼簡單；很多時候，它同時也關乎方便、體驗，以及那種可以為自己作選擇的小小樂趣。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say shopping is still a very popular
activity in Hong Kong, although the way people shop is becoming
more diverse. It is no longer just about carrying bags home; quite
often, it is also about convenience, experience, and the small
pleasure of choosing for yourself.
[總結來說，我會說購物在香港仍然是一項很受歡迎的活動，只不過人們購物的方式正變得
更多元。它不再只是把購物袋帶回家那麼簡單；很多時候，它同時也關乎方便、體驗，以及
那種可以為自己作選擇的小小樂趣。]

question.english: How have shopping habits changed over recent years?
question.chinese: 近年來，人們的購物習慣有甚麼改變？

source_lines.text: Topic: How have shopping habits changed over recent years?
近年來，人們的購物習慣有甚麼改變？

components.english: Over recent years, shopping habits have become much more digital, convenient, and fast-paced than they used to be.
components.chinese: 近年來，人們的購物習慣已經變得比以前數碼化得多，也更講求方便，而且節奏更快。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Over recent years, shopping habits have become much more
digital, convenient, and fast-paced than they used to be.
[近年來，人們的購物習慣已經變得比以前數碼化得多，也更講求方便，而且節奏更快。]

components.english: The main reason is that online shopping platforms, delivery services, and mobile payment systems have made the whole process smoother than ever. People no longer need to walk from shop to shop just to compare prices or look for a particular product. Instead, they can scroll, compare, tap, and check out within minutes. In that sense, shopping has shifted from something people deliberately set aside time for to something they can do almost in the cracks of daily life.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，網購平台、送貨服務，以及電子支付系統，已經令整個購物過程比以前順暢得多。人們不再需要一家一家店慢慢逛，只為了比較價錢，或者找某一件商品。相反，現在很多時只需要滑一滑、比一比、按幾下，很快就能完成購買。從這個角度來看，購物已經由一件需要特意騰出時間去做的事情，變成一件幾乎可以塞進日常生活縫隙裡隨時完成的事。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that online shopping platforms,
delivery services, and mobile payment systems have made the whole
process smoother than ever. People no longer need to walk from
shop to shop just to compare prices or look for a particular product.
Instead, they can scroll, compare, tap, and check out within minutes.
In that sense, shopping has shifted from something people
deliberately set aside time for to something they can do almost in the
cracks of daily life.
[主要原因是，網購平台、送貨服務，以及電子支付系統，已經令整個購物過程比以前順暢得
多。人們不再需要一家一家店慢慢逛，只為了比較價錢，或者找某一件商品。相反，現在很
多時只需要滑一滑、比一比、按幾下，很快就能完成購買。從這個角度來看，購物已經由一
件需要特意騰出時間去做的事情，變成一件幾乎可以塞進日常生活縫隙裡隨時完成的事。]

components.english: For example, if someone wants to buy clothes, skincare products, or even groceries, they can now do it on their phone while sitting on the bus or during a lunch break. In a place like Hong Kong, where life moves quickly, that kind of convenience is very appealing.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人想買衣服、護膚品，甚至日常雜貨，他現在都可以在巴士上，或者午飯休息時，直接用手機完成購買。在像香港這樣生活節奏很快的地方，這種方便感其實非常吸引。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone wants to buy clothes, skincare
products, or even groceries, they can now do it on their phone while
sitting on the bus or during a lunch break. In a place like Hong Kong,
where life moves quickly, that kind of convenience is very appealing.
[例如，如果一個人想買衣服、護膚品，甚至日常雜貨，他現在都可以在巴士上，或者午飯休
息時，直接用手機完成購買。在像香港這樣生活節奏很快的地方，這種方便感其實非常吸

components.english: So, one very obvious change is that shopping has become far more online and on-demand, rather than mainly based on visiting physical shops.
components.chinese: 所以，一個非常明顯的改變就是：購物已經變得更加依賴網上平台，也更即時化，而不再主要建立在親身去實體店這件事上。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one very obvious change is that shopping has
become far more online and on-demand, rather than mainly based on
visiting physical shops.
[所以，一個非常明顯的改變就是：購物已經變得更加依賴網上平台，也更即時化，而不再主
要建立在親身去實體店這件事上。]

components.english: On top of that, I think people have also become more price-conscious and more research-driven when they shop.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為人們現在購物時，普遍比以前更重視價錢，也更習慣先做資料搜集。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think people have also become more
price-conscious and more research-driven when they shop.
[除此之外，我也認為人們現在購物時，普遍比以前更重視價錢，也更習慣先做資料搜集。]

components.english: To be honest, many shoppers now read reviews, compare websites, wait for discounts, and think twice before spending money. Rising living costs have made people more careful, while social media has also changed the way products are discovered and promoted. So shopping today is a curious mix of caution and temptation: people want the best deal possible, but they are also constantly being lured by trends, influencers, and targeted ads.
components.chinese: 老實說，現在很多消費者都會先看評價、比較不同網站、等減價，並且在花錢之前多想一層。生活成本上升，令大家對開支更加小心；與此同時，社交媒體也改變了人們發現產品和接收推廣的方式。所以，今天的購物其實是一種很微妙的混合：一方面，人們會小心翼翼地想找最划算的交易；但另一方面，他們又一直被潮流、網絡紅人，以及精準廣告吸引着。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, many shoppers now read reviews,
compare websites, wait for discounts, and think twice before
spending money. Rising living costs have made people more careful,
while social media has also changed the way products are discovered
and promoted. So shopping today is a curious mix of caution and
temptation: people want the best deal possible, but they are also
constantly being lured by trends, influencers, and targeted ads.
[老實說，現在很多消費者都會先看評價、比較不同網站、等減價，並且在花錢之前多想一
層。生活成本上升，令大家對開支更加小心；與此同時，社交媒體也改變了人們發現產品和
接收推廣的方式。所以，今天的購物其實是一種很微妙的混合：一方面，人們會小心翼翼地
想找最划算的交易；但另一方面，他們又一直被潮流、網絡紅人，以及精準廣告吸引着。]

components.english: For instance, someone may first notice a product on social media, then check several platforms, read customer comments, and wait for a sale before finally buying it. It is also quite common now for people to try something in a physical shop but order it online later if the price is better.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能先在社交媒體上看到某件產品，之後再去不同平台比較、看買家評論，甚至等到減價時才真正下單。現在也很常見的是，人們先在實體店試過某樣東西，之後如果發現網上價格更好，就改為在網上購買。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone may first notice a product on
social media, then check several platforms, read customer comments,
and wait for a sale before finally buying it. It is also quite common
now for people to try something in a physical shop but order it online
later if the price is better.
[例如，一個人可能先在社交媒體上看到某件產品，之後再去不同平台比較、看買家評論，甚
至等到減價時才真正下單。現在也很常見的是，人們先在實體店試過某樣東西，之後如果發
現網上價格更好，就改為在網上購買。]

components.english: Overall, I would say shopping habits have become more digital, more informed, and more convenience-led, but also more influenced by prices and online trends. People still shop, of course, but now they often do it with a phone in one hand and a comparison mindset in the other.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說人們的購物習慣已經變得更數碼化、更有資訊意識，也更受方便性主導；不過同時，它也更受到價格和網上潮流的影響。人們當然仍然在購物，只不過現在很多時都是一手拿着手機，一手帶着比較的心態在買東西。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say shopping habits have become
more digital, more informed, and more convenience-led, but also
more influenced by prices and online trends. People still shop, of
course, but now they often do it with a phone in one hand and a
comparison mindset in the other.
[總結來說，我會說人們的購物習慣已經變得更數碼化、更有資訊意識，也更受方便性主導；
不過同時，它也更受到價格和網上潮流的影響。人們當然仍然在購物，只不過現在很多時都
是一手拿着手機，一手帶着比較的心態在買東西。]

question.english: To what extent do you think advertising affects the way people shop?
question.chinese: 你認為廣告在多大程度上影響人們的購物方式？

source_lines.text: To what extent do you think advertising affects the way people shop?
你認為廣告在多大程度上影響人們的購物方式？

components.english: I think advertising affects the way people shop to a fairly large extent, although it does not control people completely.
components.chinese: 我認為，廣告在相當大的程度上會影響人們的購物方式，不過它並不能完全操控人。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think advertising affects the way people shop to a fairly large
extent, although it does not control people completely.
[我認為，廣告在相當大的程度上會影響人們的購物方式，不過它並不能完全操控人。]

components.english: The main reason is that advertising shapes attention before it shapes decisions. It puts certain products in front of people again and again, makes brands feel familiar, and often links shopping with emotion, identity, or lifestyle. In that sense, advertising does not always drag people to the shop; more often, it plants a seed in the mind. Later, when people are deciding what to buy, that seed can quietly influence them.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，廣告很多時候是先影響人的注意力，然後才影響決定。它會不斷把某些產品放到人們眼前，令品牌變得熟悉，並且常常把購物和情感、身份認同，或者生活方式連結起來。從這個角度來說，廣告未必總是直接把人拉去購物；更多時候，它像是在心裡先種下一顆種子。等到人真正要作出購買決定時，那顆種子就可能悄悄地發揮作用。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that advertising shapes attention
before it shapes decisions. It puts certain products in front of people
again and again, makes brands feel familiar, and often links shopping
with emotion, identity, or lifestyle. In that sense, advertising does not
always drag people to the shop; more often, it plants a seed in the
mind. Later, when people are deciding what to buy, that seed can
quietly influence them.
[主要原因是，廣告很多時候是先影響人的注意力，然後才影響決定。它會不斷把某些產品
放到人們眼前，令品牌變得熟悉，並且常常把購物和情感、身份認同，或者生活方式連結起
來。從這個角度來說，廣告未必總是直接把人拉去購物；更多時候，它像是在心裡先種下一
顆種子。等到人真正要作出購買決定時，那顆種子就可能悄悄地發揮作用。]

components.english: For example, if someone keeps seeing advertisements for a particular skincare brand, mobile phone, or snack on social media, they may start to feel that the product is popular, trustworthy, or somehow better than the alternatives, even before they have tried it. When they finally go shopping, that brand is likely to come to mind first.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人不停在社交媒體上看到某個護膚品牌、手機，或者零食的廣告，他很可能會在還未試過那件產品之前，就開始覺得它很受歡迎、值得信賴，或者好像比其他選擇更好。等到他真的去購物時，那個品牌就很可能最先浮現在腦海裡。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone keeps seeing advertisements for
a particular skincare brand, mobile phone, or snack on social media,
they may start to feel that the product is popular, trustworthy, or
somehow better than the alternatives, even before they have tried it.
When they finally go shopping, that brand is likely to come to mind
first.
[例如，如果一個人不停在社交媒體上看到某個護膚品牌、手機，或者零食的廣告，他很可
能會在還未試過那件產品之前，就開始覺得它很受歡迎、值得信賴，或者好像比其他選擇更
好。等到他真的去購物時，那個品牌就很可能最先浮現在腦海裡。]

components.english: So, to a large extent, advertising does affect the way people shop, because it influences what they notice, what they remember, and what they begin to want.
components.chinese: 所以，從很大程度上來說，廣告的確會影響人們的購物方式，因為它會影響人們注意到甚麼、記得甚麼，以及開始想要甚麼。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to a large extent, advertising does affect the way
people shop, because it influences what they notice, what they
remember, and what they begin to want.
[所以，從很大程度上來說，廣告的確會影響人們的購物方式，因為它會影響人們注意到甚
麼、記得甚麼，以及開始想要甚麼。]

components.english: That said, I do not think advertising is all-powerful, because people are not robots and shopping decisions are still shaped by reality.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為廣告是無所不能的，因為人終究不是機器，而購物決定始終還是會受到現實因素影響。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think advertising is all-powerful, because
people are not robots and shopping decisions are still shaped by
reality.
[不過，我並不認為廣告是無所不能的，因為人終究不是機器，而購物決定始終還是會受到
現實因素影響。]

components.english: Price, personal needs, product quality, and recommendations from friends or family still matter a great deal. An advertisement may get its foot in the door, but it cannot always close the deal. If something is too expensive, badly reviewed, or simply unnecessary, many people will think twice. In other words, advertising can nudge people, but it cannot completely override common sense.
components.chinese: 價格、個人需要、產品質素，以及朋友家人的推薦，仍然非常重要。廣告也許能先打開人心裡的一道門，但它未必總能完成最後成交。如果某件東西太貴、評價不好，或者根本不需要，很多人還是會再想一想。換句話說，廣告可以推人一把，但它不能完全蓋過人的常識。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Price, personal needs, product quality, and
recommendations from friends or family still matter a great deal. An
advertisement may get its foot in the door, but it cannot always close
the deal. If something is too expensive, badly reviewed, or simply
unnecessary, many people will think twice. In other words, advertising
can nudge people, but it cannot completely override common sense.
[價格、個人需要、產品質素，以及朋友家人的推薦，仍然非常重要。廣告也許能先打開人心
裡的一道門，但它未必總能完成最後成交。如果某件東西太貴、評價不好，或者根本不需要
，很多人還是會再想一想。換句話說，廣告可以推人一把，但它不能完全蓋過人的常識。]

components.english: For instance, a person may be drawn to a beautifully advertised pair of shoes, but if the price is unreasonable or the quality feels poor when they try it on, they may walk away without buying it. So the advertisement may spark the interest, but the final decision still depends on other factors.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人也許會被一雙廣告拍得很吸引的鞋子吸引住，但如果價格不合理，或者試穿後覺得品質不好，他最後還是可能不會買。所以，廣告也許能點燃興趣，但最終決定仍然取決於其他因素。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a person may be drawn to a beautifully
advertised pair of shoes, but if the price is unreasonable or the quality
feels poor when they try it on, they may walk away without buying it.
So the advertisement may spark the interest, but the final decision
still depends on other factors.
[例如，一個人也許會被一雙廣告拍得很吸引的鞋子吸引住，但如果價格不合理，或者試穿
後覺得品質不好，他最後還是可能不會買。所以，廣告也許能點燃興趣，但最終決定仍然取
決於其他因素。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say advertising has a strong influence on the way people shop, especially by shaping desire and directing attention. However, advertising is more like a powerful push than a string that can control people at will; it may bring people to the door, but when it comes to actually paying, reality usually has the final say.
components.chinese: 總結來說，廣告對人們的購物方式確實有很強的影響，尤其是在塑造欲望和引導注意力方面。不過，廣告比較像是一股很有力的推動，而不是一根可以隨意操控人的線；它能把人帶到門口，但到了要付錢的那一刻，現實往往才是最後說話的人。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say advertising has a strong influence on
the way people shop, especially by shaping desire and directing
attention. However, advertising is more like a powerful push than a
string that can control people at will; it may bring people to the door,
but when it comes to actually paying, reality usually has the final say.
[總結來說，廣告對人們的購物方式確實有很強的影響，尤其是在塑造欲望和引導注意力方
面。不過，廣告比較像是一股很有力的推動，而不是一根可以隨意操控人的線；它能把人帶
到門口，但到了要付錢的那一刻，現實往往才是最後說話的人。]

question.english: Do you think shopping habits are likely to change in the future?
question.chinese: 你認為未來人們的購物習慣有可能改變嗎？

source_lines.text: Do you think shopping habits are likely to change in the future?
你認為未來人們的購物習慣有可能改變嗎？

components.english: Yes, definitely. I think shopping habits are very likely to change quite a lot in the future.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對會。我認為未來人們的購物習慣很大機會會有相當大的改變。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, definitely. I think shopping habits are very likely to
change quite a lot in the future.
[是的，絕對會。我認為未來人們的購物習慣很大機會會有相當大的改變。]

components.english: The main reason is that technology is already changing the way people buy things. Shopping is becoming faster, easier, and more personalised. People can compare prices, read reviews, watch short videos about a product, and place an order within minutes. In a way, the shopfront is slowly moving from the street to the screen.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，科技其實已經在改變人們買東西的方式。購物正變得更快、更方便，也更個人化。人們可以在幾分鐘內比較價格、看評價、看產品短片，然後直接下單。某程度上，商店的櫥窗其實正慢慢由街上搬到螢幕裡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that technology is already
changing the way people buy things. Shopping is becoming faster,
easier, and more personalised. People can compare prices, read
reviews, watch short videos about a product, and place an order
within minutes. In a way, the shopfront is slowly moving from the
street to the screen.
[主要原因是，科技其實已經在改變人們買東西的方式。購物正變得更快、更方便，也更個
人化。人們可以在幾分鐘內比較價格、看評價、看產品短片，然後直接下單。某程度上，商店
的櫥窗其實正慢慢由街上搬到螢幕裡。]

components.english: For example, if someone sees a jacket or a pair of headphones online, they may check a few reviews, compare two or three websites, and buy it the same evening without ever going to a physical shop.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人在網上看到一件外套或一副耳機，他可能只會看看幾條評價、比較兩三個網站，然後當晚就買下來，甚至完全不用走進實體店。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone sees a jacket or a pair of
headphones online, they may check a few reviews, compare two or
three websites, and buy it the same evening without ever going to a
physical shop.
[例如，如果一個人在網上看到一件外套或一副耳機，他可能只會看看幾條評價、比較兩三
個網站，然後當晚就買下來，甚至完全不用走進實體店。]

components.english: So yes, I would say future shopping will probably become more digital, more immediate, and more convenience-driven.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說未來的購物很可能會變得更數碼化、更即時，也更以方便為主。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say future shopping will probably
become more digital, more immediate, and more convenience-driven.
[所以，是的，我會說未來的購物很可能會變得更數碼化、更即時，也更以方便為主。]

components.english: That said, I do not think traditional shopping will disappear completely.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為傳統購物會完全消失。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think traditional shopping will disappear
completely.
[不過，我並不認為傳統購物會完全消失。]

components.english: Some things are still easier to buy in person. If people are buying clothes, shoes, furniture, or expensive items, they often want to see them with their own eyes or try them out first. On top of that, shopping is not always just about buying; sometimes it is also about the experience, the atmosphere, and spending time with other people. So I think the future will be more mixed than extreme.
components.chinese: 有些東西始終還是親身去買會比較容易。如果人們要買衣服、鞋子、家具，或者一些較昂貴的物品，他們很多時候仍然想親眼看看，或者先親自試一試。除此之外，購物有時也不只是為了買東西；它同時也關乎那種體驗、氣氛，以及和別人一起花時間的感覺。所以，我認為未來會更像是一種混合模式，而不是走向某個極端。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Some things are still easier to buy in person. If
people are buying clothes, shoes, furniture, or expensive items, they
often want to see them with their own eyes or try them out first. On
top of that, shopping is not always just about buying; sometimes it is
also about the experience, the atmosphere, and spending time with
other people. So I think the future will be more mixed than extreme.
[有些東西始終還是親身去買會比較容易。如果人們要買衣服、鞋子、家具，或者一些較昂
貴的物品，他們很多時候仍然想親眼看看，或者先親自試一試。除此之外，購物有時也不只
是為了買東西；它同時也關乎那種體驗、氣氛，以及和別人一起花時間的感覺。所以，我認
為未來會更像是一種混合模式，而不是走向某個極端。]

components.english: For instance, someone may go to a shop to try on a pair of shoes, but later order them online because the price is better. That kind of blend between online and offline shopping may become more and more common.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能會先去店裡試一雙鞋，但之後再到網上買，因為價格比較划算。這種把線上和線下購物混合起來的做法，未來很可能會愈來愈常見。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, someone may go to a shop to try on a pair
of shoes, but later order them online because the price is better. That
kind of blend between online and offline shopping may become more
and more common.
[例如，一個人可能會先去店裡試一雙鞋，但之後再到網上買，因為價格比較划算。這種把
線上和線下購物混合起來的做法，未來很可能會愈來愈常見。]

components.english: Overall, I do think shopping habits will change in the future, but not in a one-way fashion. People will probably shop more online and more intelligently, while still keeping physical shops for trust, experience, and certain kinds of products.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我的確認為未來的購物習慣會改變，但不會只是單向地改變。人們很可能會更常在網上購物，也會買得更聰明；不過，實體店仍然會因為信任感、購物體驗，以及某些特定商品而保留它的地位。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I do think shopping habits will change in the
future, but not in a one-way fashion. People will probably shop more
online and more intelligently, while still keeping physical shops for
trust, experience, and certain kinds of products.
[總結來說，我的確認為未來的購物習慣會改變，但不會只是單向地改變。人們很可能會更
常在網上購物，也會買得更聰明；不過，實體店仍然會因為信任感、購物體驗，以及某些特
定商品而保留它的地位。]

question.english: Why do some people prefer to purchase brand name products produced abroad?
question.chinese: 為甚麼有些人較喜歡購買在外國生產的名牌產品？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do some people prefer to purchase brand name products
produced abroad?
為甚麼有些人較喜歡購買在外國生產的名牌產品？

components.english: I think one major reason is that people often associate foreign brand-name products with better quality, stronger reliability, and a greater sense of trust.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個主要原因是，人們往往會把外國生產的名牌產品和較高品質、更穩定的表現，以及更強的信任感連在一起。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one major reason is that people often associate foreign
brand-name products with better quality, stronger reliability, and a
greater sense of trust.
[我認為，其中一個主要原因是，人們往往會把外國生產的名牌產品和較高品質、更穩定的
表現，以及更強的信任感連在一起。]

components.english: Fairly or unfairly, many people feel that well-known overseas brands have stricter standards, better craftsmanship, or more consistent quality control. When people are spending a lot of money, they usually want peace of mind as much as the product itself. In that sense, a foreign brand label can feel a bit like a promise before the box is even opened.
components.chinese: 無論這種想法是否完全公平，很多人都會覺得知名的海外品牌有更嚴格的標準、更好的工藝，或者更穩定的品質控制。當人們花一大筆錢時，他們很多時不只是想買到產品本身，也想買到一份安心感。從這個角度來看，一個外國名牌標籤有時就像一個承諾，甚至在盒子還未打開之前，就已經先替產品說話了。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Fairly or unfairly, many people feel that well-known
overseas brands have stricter standards, better craftsmanship, or
more consistent quality control. When people are spending a lot of
money, they usually want peace of mind as much as the product itself.
In that sense, a foreign brand label can feel a bit like a promise before
the box is even opened.
[無論這種想法是否完全公平，很多人都會覺得知名的海外品牌有更嚴格的標準、更好的工
藝，或者更穩定的品質控制。當人們花一大筆錢時，他們很多時不只是想買到產品本身，也
想買到一份安心感。從這個角度來看，一個外國名牌標籤有時就像一個承諾，甚至在盒子還
未打開之前，就已經先替產品說話了。]

components.english: For example, if someone is buying a watch, a handbag, skincare, or even baby products, they may lean towards a famous foreign brand because they feel it is less likely to disappoint them. Even if the local version is cheaper, they may still prefer the imported one because it gives them more confidence.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人要買手錶、手袋、護膚品，甚至嬰兒用品，他可能會傾向選擇一個知名的外國品牌，因為他覺得那樣較不容易令自己失望。即使本地版本較便宜，他仍然可能會偏向選擇進口的那一款，因為那會令他更有信心。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone is buying a watch, a handbag,
skincare, or even baby products, they may lean towards a famous
foreign brand because they feel it is less likely to disappoint them.
Even if the local version is cheaper, they may still prefer the imported
one because it gives them more confidence.
[例如，如果一個人要買手錶、手袋、護膚品，甚至嬰兒用品，他可能會傾向選擇一個知名的
外國品牌，因為他覺得那樣較不容易令自己失望。即使本地版本較便宜，他仍然可能會偏向
選擇進口的那一款，因為那會令他更有信心。]

components.english: So, one clear reason is that people often believe foreign brand-name products are safer bets, especially when the price is high and expectations are even higher.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一個很明顯的原因就是，人們常常會覺得外國名牌產品是較穩妥的選擇，特別是當價錢不低，而期望又更高的時候。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear reason is that people often believe
foreign brand-name products are safer bets, especially when the price
is high and expectations are even higher.
[所以，其中一個很明顯的原因就是，人們常常會覺得外國名牌產品是較穩妥的選擇，特別
是當價錢不低，而期望又更高的時候。]

components.english: On top of that, I think some people buy these products not only for practical reasons, but also for image, identity, and a certain sense of prestige.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為，有些人購買這類產品，不只是出於實際用途，也和形象、身份認同，以及某種體面感有關。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think some people buy these products not
only for practical reasons, but also for image, identity, and a certain
sense of prestige.
[除此之外，我也認為，有些人購買這類產品，不只是出於實際用途，也和形象、身份認同，
以及某種體面感有關。]

components.english: To be honest, people do not always buy brand-name products purely with logic. Sometimes they are buying the feeling wrapped around the object. A product made abroad, especially by a famous brand, can seem more stylish, more exclusive, or simply more desirable. It may signal taste, success, or a certain lifestyle. In other words, some people are not just buying a thing; they are buying the story and status attached to it.
components.chinese: 老實說，人們購買名牌產品很多時並不完全是出於理性。有時候，他們其實是在購買包裹在那件物品外面的感覺。一件在外國生產、而且來自知名品牌的產品，往往會顯得更有型、更有獨特感，或者單純更令人嚮往。它可能象徵品味、成就，或者某種生活方式。換句話說，有些人買下的不只是東西本身，而是附在它身上的故事和地位感。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, people do not always buy brand-name
products purely with logic. Sometimes they are buying the feeling
wrapped around the object. A product made abroad, especially by a
famous brand, can seem more stylish, more exclusive, or simply more
desirable. It may signal taste, success, or a certain lifestyle. In other
words, some people are not just buying a thing; they are buying the
story and status attached to it.
[老實說，人們購買名牌產品很多時並不完全是出於理性。有時候，他們其實是在購買包裹
在那件物品外面的感覺。一件在外國生產、而且來自知名品牌的產品，往往會顯得更有型、
更有獨特感，或者單純更令人嚮往。它可能象徵品味、成就，或者某種生活方式。換句話說，
有些人買下的不只是東西本身，而是附在它身上的故事和地位感。]

components.english: For instance, someone may choose an imported designer bag or a pair of branded shoes not because they urgently need it, but because it makes them feel polished, confident, or socially recognised. The item becomes more than an object; it becomes a kind of statement.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人選擇一個外國設計師品牌的手袋，或者一雙名牌鞋，未必是因為他真的急需它，而可能是因為那樣會令他覺得自己更精緻、更有自信，或者更容易得到某種社會認可。那件東西於是就不再只是物品，而變成了一種表態。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone may choose an imported designer
bag or a pair of branded shoes not because they urgently need it, but
because it makes them feel polished, confident, or socially
recognised. The item becomes more than an object; it becomes a kind
of statement.
[例如，一個人選擇一個外國設計師品牌的手袋，或者一雙名牌鞋，未必是因為他真的急需
它，而可能是因為那樣會令他覺得自己更精緻、更有自信，或者更容易得到某種社會認可。
那件東西於是就不再只是物品，而變成了一種表態。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say some people prefer foreign brand-name products because they see them as more trustworthy, more refined, and sometimes more symbolic of the image they want to project. Sometimes it is about quality, and sometimes it is about identity; very often, it is a mixture of both.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，有些人偏好外國生產的名牌產品，是因為他們會把這些產品看成更值得信任、更精緻，而且有時更能代表自己想呈現的形象。有時那關乎品質，有時則關乎身份認同；而很多時候，其實是兩者混合在一起。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say some people prefer foreign
brand-name products because they see them as more trustworthy,
more refined, and sometimes more symbolic of the image they want
to project. Sometimes it is about quality, and sometimes it is about
identity; very often, it is a mixture of both.
[總結來說，我會說，有些人偏好外國生產的名牌產品，是因為他們會把這些產品看成更值
得信任、更精緻，而且有時更能代表自己想呈現的形象。有時那關乎品質，有時則關乎身份
認同；而很多時候，其實是兩者混合在一起。]

question.english: Is service better in large shops or in small shops?
question.chinese: 大型商店的服務比較好，還是小店的服務比較好？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is service better in large shops or in small shops?
[大型商店的服務比較好，還是小店的服務比較好？]

components.english: Generally speaking, I would say service is often better in small shops, especially if we are talking about personal attention and friendliness.
components.chinese: 一般來說，如果我們談的是個人化照顧和親切感，我會說小店的服務往往比較好。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Generally speaking, I would say service is often better in
small shops, especially if we are talking about personal attention and
friendliness.
[一般來說，如果我們談的是個人化照顧和親切感，我會說小店的服務往往比較好。]

components.english: The main reason is that small shops usually feel more human. The staff are often more approachable, they may know their products inside out, and they sometimes take more time with each customer. In a small shop, service can feel less like a script and more like a real conversation. Because every customer matters, the staff often go the extra mile to be helpful.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，小店通常更有人情味。店員很多時候比較容易接近，也可能對自己賣的產品非常熟悉，而且有時會願意花更多時間照顧每一位客人。在小店裡，服務與其說像照本宣科，不如說更像一場真正的交流。正因為每一位客人都很重要，店員往往會更願意多走一步去幫忙。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that small shops usually feel more
human. The staff are often more approachable, they may know their
products inside out, and they sometimes take more time with each
customer. In a small shop, service can feel less like a script and more
like a real conversation. Because every customer matters, the staff
often go the extra mile to be helpful.
[主要原因是，小店通常更有人情味。店員很多時候比較容易接近，也可能對自己賣的產品
非常熟悉，而且有時會願意花更多時間照顧每一位客人。在小店裡，服務與其說像照本宣科
，不如說更像一場真正的交流。正因為每一位客人都很重要，店員往往會更願意多走一步去
幫忙。]

components.english: For example, if you go to a small bookshop, bakery, or local clothing store, the owner may remember what you bought before, recommend something that actually suits you, or answer your questions with real patience. That kind of service feels warm rather than mechanical.
components.chinese: 例如，如果你去一間小書店、麵包店，或者本地服裝店，店主可能會記得你之前買過甚麼，推薦真正適合你的東西，或者很有耐性地回答你的問題。那種服務給人的感覺是有溫度的，而不是機械式的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if you go to a small bookshop, bakery, or
local clothing store, the owner may remember what you bought
before, recommend something that actually suits you, or answer your
questions with real patience. That kind of service feels warm rather
than mechanical.
[例如，如果你去一間小書店、麵包店，或者本地服裝店，店主可能會記得你之前買過甚麼，
推薦真正適合你的東西，或者很有耐性地回答你的問題。那種服務給人的感覺是有溫度的，
而不是機械式的。]

components.english: So, if I had to choose, I would say small shops often have the edge when it comes to warmth, patience, and personal service.
components.chinese: 所以，如果一定要選一邊，我會說小店在溫度感、耐性，以及個人化服務方面，往往更有優勢。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to choose, I would say small shops often
have the edge when it comes to warmth, patience, and personal
service.
[所以，如果一定要選一邊，我會說小店在溫度感、耐性，以及個人化服務方面，往往更有優

components.english: That said, large shops can sometimes offer better service in a different sense, because they are often more efficient, more professional, and more system-based.
components.chinese: 不過，大型商店有時也會在另一個層面上提供更好的服務，因為它們往往更有效率、更專業，而且有更完整的制度。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, large shops can sometimes offer better service in
a different sense, because they are often more efficient, more
professional, and more system-based.
[不過，大型商店有時也會在另一個層面上提供更好的服務，因為它們往往更有效率、更專
業，而且有更完整的制度。]

components.english: In big shops, staff are usually trained in a more standardised way, so the service may be more consistent. They may also have clearer return policies, better after-sales support, and more staff available when the shop is busy. In other words, large shops may not always feel as personal, but they can feel more reliable. Small shops may win the heart, while large shops often win on structure.
components.chinese: 在大型商店裡，店員通常接受過更標準化的訓練，所以服務表現可能會更一致。它們也可能有更清晰的退換貨政策、更完善的售後支援，以及在繁忙時段有更多人手。換句話說，大型商店未必總是那麼有個人味，但它們往往會讓人覺得更穩陣。小店也許較容易打動人心，而大店則常常贏在制度。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: In big shops, staff are usually trained in a more
standardised way, so the service may be more consistent. They may
also have clearer return policies, better after-sales support, and more
staff available when the shop is busy. In other words, large shops may
not always feel as personal, but they can feel more reliable. Small
shops may win the heart, while large shops often win on structure.
[在大型商店裡，店員通常接受過更標準化的訓練，所以服務表現可能會更一致。它們也可
能有更清晰的退換貨政策、更完善的售後支援，以及在繁忙時段有更多人手。換句話說，大
型商店未必總是那麼有個人味，但它們往往會讓人覺得更穩陣。小店也許較容易打動人心，
而大店則常常贏在制度。]

components.english: For instance, if someone is buying electronics or furniture, they may prefer a large shop because it usually offers official warranties, delivery arrangements, and a more organised customer service system. In that case, “better service” may mean peace of mind rather than personal charm.
components.chinese: 例如，如果有人要買電子產品或家具，他可能會更傾向去大型商店，因為那裡通常會提供正式保養、送貨安排，以及較有組織的客戶服務系統。在這種情況下，「更好的服務」可能代表的是安心感，而不只是個人魅力。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if someone is buying electronics or
furniture, they may prefer a large shop because it usually offers
official warranties, delivery arrangements, and a more organised
customer service system. In that case, “better service” may mean
peace of mind rather than personal charm.
[例如，如果有人要買電子產品或家具，他可能會更傾向去大型商店，因為那裡通常會提供
正式保養、送貨安排，以及較有組織的客戶服務系統。在這種情況下，「更好的服務」可能代
表的是安心感，而不只是個人魅力。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say small shops often provide better personal service, while large shops may provide better formal service. So the answer really depends on what kind of service you value more: the human touch or the smooth system.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說小店通常提供更好的個人化服務，而大型商店則可能提供更好的制度化服務。所以，答案其實取決於你更重視哪一種服務：人情味，還是運作流暢的系統。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say small shops often provide better
personal service, while large shops may provide better formal service.
So the answer really depends on what kind of service you value more:
the human touch or the smooth system.
[總結來說，我會說小店通常提供更好的個人化服務，而大型商店則可能提供更好的制度化
服務。所以，答案其實取決於你更重視哪一種服務：人情味，還是運作流暢的系統。]

question.english: What do people do when they get bad service?
question.chinese: 當人們遇到服務差的情況時，通常會怎樣做？

source_lines.text: Topic: What do people do when they get bad service?
當人們遇到服務差的情況時，通常會怎樣做？

components.english: I’d say most people usually do one of two things: they either complain and ask for the problem to be fixed, or they quietly decide not to come back.
components.chinese: 我會說，大多數人通常會做兩件事中的其中一件：要麼投訴並要求對方處理問題，要麼就安靜地決定下次不再光顧。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say most people usually do one of two things: they either
complain and ask for the problem to be fixed, or they quietly decide
not to come back.
[我會說，大多數人通常會做兩件事中的其中一件：要麼投訴並要求對方處理問題，要麼就
安靜地決定下次不再光顧。]

components.english: The reason is quite simple. If people are paying for a service, they usually expect basic respect, efficiency, and a certain standard of treatment. So when the service is rude, careless, or painfully slow, it tends to leave a sour taste in the mouth. Most people do not want to make a scene for no reason, but they also do not want to just grin and bear it if they feel badly treated.
components.chinese: 原因其實很簡單。如果人們有付錢購買服務，他們通常都會期望得到最基本的尊重、效率，以及一定水平的對待。所以當服務態度很差、很馬虎，或者慢得令人受不了時，往往就會讓人心裡很不是味兒。大多數人並不是想無端端把場面鬧大，但如果他們覺得自己被怠慢了，也不會願意只是硬吞下去。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is quite simple. If people are paying for a
service, they usually expect basic respect, efficiency, and a certain
standard of treatment. So when the service is rude, careless, or
painfully slow, it tends to leave a sour taste in the mouth. Most people
do not want to make a scene for no reason, but they also do not want
to just grin and bear it if they feel badly treated.
[原因其實很簡單。如果人們有付錢購買服務，他們通常都會期望得到最基本的尊重、效率
，以及一定水平的對待。所以當服務態度很差、很馬虎，或者慢得令人受不了時，往往就會
讓人心裡很不是味兒。大多數人並不是想無端端把場面鬧大，但如果他們覺得自己被怠慢
了，也不會願意只是硬吞下去。]

components.english: For example, if someone is in a restaurant and the staff are rude or the order is wrong, they may politely call the waiter over, explain the problem, and ask for it to be corrected. If the staff handle it well, the customer may calm down quite quickly.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人在餐廳裡遇到店員態度差，或者送錯餐點，他可能就會有禮貌地叫侍應過來，說明問題，並要求對方改正。如果店員處理得好，客人其實通常也很快會冷靜下來。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone is in a restaurant and the staff
are rude or the order is wrong, they may politely call the waiter over,
explain the problem, and ask for it to be corrected. If the staff handle
it well, the customer may calm down quite quickly.
[例如，如果一個人在餐廳裡遇到店員態度差，或者送錯餐點，他可能就會有禮貌地叫侍應
過來，說明問題，並要求對方改正。如果店員處理得好，客人其實通常也很快會冷靜下來。]

components.english: So, in many cases, people first try to solve the problem on the spot, because that is often the most direct and practical response.
components.chinese: 所以，在很多情況下，人們第一步都會先嘗試當場解決問題，因為那通常是最直接、也最實際的做法。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in many cases, people first try to solve the problem
on the spot, because that is often the most direct and practical
response.
[所以，在很多情況下，人們第一步都會先嘗試當場解決問題，因為那通常是最直接、也最
實際的做法。]

components.english: That said, not everyone complains openly. Some people would rather avoid confrontation and simply walk away.
components.chinese: 不過，也不是每個人都會直接開口投訴。有些人反而寧願避免正面衝突，然後乾脆離開。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, not everyone complains openly. Some people
would rather avoid confrontation and simply walk away.
[不過，也不是每個人都會直接開口投訴。有些人反而寧願避免正面衝突，然後乾脆離開。]

components.english: I think that depends a lot on personality. Some people are very direct and will speak their mind straight away, whereas others would rather keep the peace, leave quietly, and then vote with their feet. These days, another common reaction is to leave a bad review online or tell friends and family about the experience. In other words, even if people say very little in the moment, bad service can still come back to haunt a business later.
components.chinese: 我覺得這很大程度上取決於個人性格。有些人很直接，當下就會把自己的不滿說出來；但也有些人寧願維持表面平靜，安靜地離開，然後用行動表態，不再光顧。現在另一種很常見的反應，就是在網上留下負評，或者把那次經歷告訴朋友和家人。換句話說，即使人們當下沒有說太多，差服務之後仍然可能回過頭來影響一家店的名聲。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: I think that depends a lot on personality. Some
people are very direct and will speak their mind straight away,
whereas others would rather keep the peace, leave quietly, and then
vote with their feet. These days, another common reaction is to leave
a bad review online or tell friends and family about the experience. In
other words, even if people say very little in the moment, bad service
can still come back to haunt a business later.
